Questions.Text: A project is in the execution phase. Equipment and devices must be transported from the vendor's warehouse to the project site through five towns. Which stakeholders should the project manager involve in planning and executing a safe transport? (Choose 3).
Options: City councils
Project sponsor
Equipment installers
Equipment suppliers
Warehouse technician
Questions.Explanation: Project sponsor, Equipment installers and Equipment suppliers In the planning, execution, and monitoring stages, continuous evaluation, and analysis of both external and internal stakeholders. City Councils are not project stakeholders.

Questions.Text: You are the project manager that just completed the initiation phase. The project was approved, and you need to identify and analyze the stakeholders. The Project charter focused on scope and budget approval only. Which of the following can be used as inputs for stakeholders’ identification? (Choose 2)
Options: Lessons Learned
Register Templates
Mission and Vision
Industry Standards
Questions.Explanation: Lessons Learned and Register Templates Organizational process assets uses to identify stakeholders process include but not limited to: Stakeholders register templates and instructions and stakeholders registers from previous projects, Lessons learned repository with information about the preferences actions, and involvement of stakeholders.

Questions.Text: A project manager is managing a project with 34 team members from six countries. The scope of the project is to modernize a process used for almost 40 years in the organization. What criteria should the project manager consider while developing the communication plan? (Choose 3).
Options: Personal diversity
Age diversity
Cultural diversity
Skills diversity
Questions.Explanation: , and C. Personal diversity, Age diversity, and Cultural diversity (Technical) skills are not a factor in developing the communication plan. The scope of the project implies stakeholders, with personal, age, and cultural diversities. It is important the project manager creates a plan to communicate with them effectively.

Questions.Text: A project steering group has made a decision to change the vendor that will deliver a component of a project. Which of the following activities should be included in the handover? (Choose 2).
Options: The old vendor should provide training for the new one and assist with the transition.
Both vendors should provide additional incentives for staff during the transition.
The new vendor should take over activities as per the existing project plan.
The new vendor should shadow the old vendor to ensure knowledge transfer.
Questions.Explanation: The old vendor should provide training for the new one and assist with the transition and the new vendor should shadow the old vendor to ensure knowledge transfer. Option B is incorrect because very likely vendor A didn't meet acceptance criteria and staff should not be rewarded for poor performance Option C is incorrect because taking over immediately, without a transition period introduces risk. Using the existing plan developed with the old vendor is also a risk to the project. The correct approach is to ensure formal and informal knowledge transfer.

Questions.Text: What would be the most effective approach to motivate your team members?
Options: Providing formal awards and recognition to the team.
Providing financial incentives to all the team members.
Providing the same incentives to all the team members.
Providing behavior-specific incentives to team members.
Questions.Explanation: Providing behavior-specific incentives to team members Employee behavior apart from performance is a key factor for rewarding and recognizing employees. Hence rewarding employees with good behavior is definitely a motivating factor for other team members too and goes a long way in building and retaining a healthy team. Hence this option is the correct answer. Formal awards are related to the power and position of the team member. It does not motivate other team members. Hence this option is incorrect Financial incentives could in fact demotivate the other team members. Hence this option is incorrect. Providing the same incentive to all team members also demotivates the other team members as they will feel that, their talents and performance are not getting recognized properly and even if they do not work properly, they will still get the same incentive as other team members. Hence this is an incorrect option.

Questions.Text: You are in the planning phase of a large project, working on developing the stakeholder management plan. You organized a one-day workshop to be sure that all the stakeholders are identified, and their power and influence are correctly assessed. Which of the following techniques will be the most suitable?
Options: Nominal Group
Power-Interest
Stakeholder Cube
Pareto Diagram
Questions.Explanation: Nominal Group The nominal group technique is the best option when the number of stakeholders is significant and they should be ranked and grouped based on power and influence

Questions.Text: You are managing a complex project with a large number of stakeholders. You decided to use the Salience Model to identify and group the most important stakeholders based on their power, urgency, and legitimacy. Which of the following aspects is the most important in this instance?
Options: Dominant
Dependent
Demanding
Definitive
Questions.Explanation: Definitive In the Salience model the Definitive group contains the stakeholder that have the highest power, urgency and legitimacy.

Questions.Text: In the Project team meeting the team members are complaining of a drop in motivation and that they don't feel that their efforts are appreciated. You decided to build and present the team as a high-performance team to the organization. Which of the following attributes of the organizational environment and managerial style will lead to a high-performance team? (Choose 3)
Options: Personal Driving Forces.
Work Challenge Dividend.
Progressive Financial Rewards.
Stimulating Environment.
Problem Solving Techniques.
Theory of Constraints.
Questions.Explanation: Personal Driving Forces, Work Challenge Dividend and Stimulating Environment Below are the right choices. •Personal Driving Forces - The degree of project success seems to be primarily determined by the strength of specific driving forces. •Work Challenge Dividend - A professionally stimulating team environment is also favorably associated with low conflict, high commitment, high work and team involvement, effective risk management, good communications, innovation, and overall team performance. •Stimulating Environment - The strongest single driver of team performance and project success is a professionally stimulating team environment, characterized by interesting and challenging work, visibility and recognition of achievements, growth potential, and good project leadership. Below are the right choices. •Progressive Financial Rewards - not a long-lasting approach •Problem Solving Techniques - is a required individual skill but not going to make a high-performance team. •Theory of Constraints - is a methodology for identifying the most important limiting factor (i.e., constraint) that stands in the way of achieving a goal and then systematically improving that constraint until it is no longer the limiting factor. In manufacturing, the constraint is often referred to as a bottleneck.

Questions.Text: After an organization trained and implemented a new mandatory project management software application for all project staff, the project manager noticed that several team members show low levels of commitment to the new software. What is the cause for this?
Options: Fear of the unknown
Lack of required skills
Lack of clear project vision
Too much communication
Questions.Explanation: Fear of the unknown Fear of the unknown is often a major reason for low commitment. Managers should investigate the potential for insecurities, determine the cause, and work with team members to reduce the anxieties created by their negative perceptions. The other answer choices do not necessarily lead to low commitment.

Questions.Text: Select three ways to improve and practice leadership skills. (Choose 3)
Options: Engage the team in the project process.
Be actively involved in the project tasks.
Treat each team member the same way.
Ask for feedback and analyze the results.
Use democracy for all team decisions.
Questions.Explanation: Engage the team in the project process, Be actively involved in the project tasks and Ask for feedback and analyze the results you need to tailor your leadership style to the member/team.

Questions.Text: A project manager who was just assigned to an information technology project found a compatibility issue with the technical infrastructure. Which step should the project manager take first to solve the problem?
Options: Examine if there is an alternate solution.
Escalate the issue to the technical infrastructure team.
Check the lessons learned database.
Consult the project sponsor about the issue.
Questions.Explanation: Check the lessons learned database Lessons learned register must be used to retrieve information from previous projects to help other projects during initiation.

Questions.Text: During project execution, a project manager is informed that critical resources will soon be removed from the project. What should the project manager do?
Options: Schedule a meeting to keep the stakeholders informed.
Inform the stakeholders at the next scheduled review meeting.
Update the risk register with this risk to project resources.
Request resource leveling.
Questions.Explanation: Schedule a meeting to keep the stakeholders informed to test candidate if they know the importance of engaging stakeholders in a timely manner. Engaging stakeholders would be the first thing to do, updating the risk register is not sufficient.

Questions.Text: A project team is in sprint 13 of a fourteen-sprint project. The project team placed a game-changing feature on the backlog for the next release that closely resembles a feature that a competitor is now launching. The product owner is insisting this game-changing feature be incorporated in the current release. What should be the next step for the project manager?
Options: Set up a review meeting with the development team to understand the magnitude of work required.
Perform the required assessments to understand the change and raise a change request with the change control board (CCB).
Meet with the product owner and project sponsor to discuss the change and decide on a way forward.
Inform the product owner that this feature cannot be included in the current project management plan, and it will be added to the next release.
Questions.Explanation: Perform the required assessments to understand the change and raise a change request with the change control board (CCB) Irrespective of the methodology, the change control process needs to be followed. Thus, the next step for the project manager is to go to the CCB for approval, before speaking with the product owner, sponsor, and development team about how to move forward with attempting to add the new feature.

Questions.Text: While reviewing progress, in the middle of an iteration, the Scrum Master points out a major risk due to a user story dependency. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Update the product backlog to include the dependency.
Update the impacted plans and monitor the risk.
Enter the risk in the iteration’s risk register.
Involve the team to complete risk analysis.
Questions.Explanation: Involve the team to complete risk analysis As the risk was identified by the Scrum Master, the next immediate step would be to involve the team in completing the risk analysis.

Questions.Text: A project manager has just received news that a new regulation has been adopted and the expectation is that that the project will be delivered compliant with this new regulation. This was previously documented as a risk item with a low probability and a moderate impact. What should the project manager do to address the issue?
Options: Revisit the risk register and update the rating and response.
Update the issue log and apply corrective action.
Revise the project management plan.
Initiate a change request for the updated regulation.
Questions.Explanation: Revisit the risk register and update the rating and response Revisiting the risk register and updating the rating and response is the correct answer. As the risk impact is changing, it must be updated in the risk register. The other answer choices are incorrect. Updating the issue log will not the solution here as the risk has not yet become an issue. Revising the project plan and initiating a change request are not relevant because the project plan and procedures are not changing.

Questions.Text: An agile team is developing a product that has no room for additional scope due to budget constraints. The customer is requesting a new independent feature be added to the solution in development. What should be the project manager's next step?
Options: Add the new feature to the product backlog.
Evaluate the new feature and collaborate with the customer.
Reject the new feature.
Reprioritize the product backlog items.
Questions.Explanation: Add the new feature to the product backlog In an agile project with budget and scope restrictions, the project manager should add the new feature to the product backlog, and address it for the next release, as budget and schedule allow. The other answer choices are incorrect because they ignore the scope and budget limits; rejecting the feature is not favorable for the customer.

Questions.Text: A project is in execution. The project manager was just reassigned to another strategic project, and the project was handed off to a new project manager. The project is currently facing issues with the quality and scheduling of some of the deliverables. What should the new project manager review first?
Options: Work breakdown structure (WBS)
Quality metrics
Stakeholder register
Risk register
Questions.Explanation: Stakeholder register As this project will touch many departments and vendors, there will be "different internal and external stakeholders". The best thing for the project manager to do is check the stakeholder register to find major requirements, expectations, and potential for stakeholders influencing project outcomes, and the phase of the project life cycle where the stakeholder has the most influence or impact.

Questions.Text: Although a project is performing within its time, scope, and budget constraints, the project manager learns that the project sponsor is unhappy with its progress. What should the project manager have done to avoid this situation?
Options: Written a project charter.
Obtained approval on project requirements.
Reviewed and confirmed the stakeholder analysis.
Developed a communications management plan.
Questions.Explanation: Reviewed and confirmed the stakeholder analysis At the beginning of the project, it is important for the project manager to understand the expectations and goals of each of the project stakeholders. These are the people who will be sending messages to the project sponsor resulting in possibly incorrect perceptions about how the project is going. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not emphasize that the project manager should have reviewed and confirmed the stakeholder analysis to avoid this situation.

Questions.Text: A project manager is addressing an impediment to a lengthy organizational process. The duration of the project is planned for 6 weeks but can be finalized within 3 weeks. What should the project manager do?
Options: Consider making work visible using Kanban.
Encourage the team to analyze the effect of the barrier.
Meet with the project sponsor to remove the setback.
Assign a team member to solve the problem.
Questions.Explanation: Consider making work visible using Kanban The project manager should consider using a Kanban board to display work and assist in taking action to remove impediments. The other answer choices are incorrect because they may impact the schedule of project.

Questions.Text: A critical project is in progress and is expected to bring a good monetary return for the firm. The firm is currently facing a cost overrun and needs liquidity to overcome the financial crisis. The ongoing project needs to be reviewed by senior management to determine whether it aligns with the organizational objectives or not. Which tool should the project manager use to prepare a report for management?
Options: Document analysis
Trend analysis
Variance analysis
Cost-benefit analysis
Questions.Explanation: Trend analysis Trend analysis is used to forecast future performance based on past results. It looks ahead in the project for expected slippages and warns the project manager ahead of time that there may be problems later in the schedule if established trends persist. This information is made available early enough in the project timeline to give the project team time to analyze and correct any anomalies. The project manager can use the results of trend analysis to recommend preventive actions if necessary. The other answer choices are incorrect because there are not the best tool for the project manager to prepare the report for management.

Questions.Text: After the transition and completion of a large equipment installation project, the project manager receives a request from the customer to add some operational cost to the project budget for team-building activities. The project was completed under budget, project contingency reserves were not spent, and the team-building activities will ensure the operational team's optimal performance. What should the project manager do?
Options: Reject the request and close the project financials.
Perform the integrated change control process.
Approve the expenditure to ensure customer satisfaction.
Document the request in the lessons learned repository.
Questions.Explanation: Reject the request and close the project financials The request should be declined as the project had been completed already and transitioned to the customer. Even if the project is in the close out phase the request should still be declined as the contingency reserves are not to be used for operational items and any other items that are not related to the project success. Financials should be closed and budget released to the organization for other business opportunities.

Questions.Text: During a daily meeting, a team member raises a concern about the timeframe planned for the deliverable prototype due to the complexity of the requirements. What two actions should the project manager take to keep the project within the timeframe? (Choose two)
Options: Analyze the impact of handling the prototype with a predictive approach.
Document the impact of an extended timeline in the issue log.
Utilize an agile approach by reviewing the backlog priorities with the stakeholders.
Modify the timeframe to add an additional iteration cycle.
Review the risk with the stakeholders and explore simplifying the requirements.
Questions.Explanation: Analyze the impact of handling the prototype with a predictive approach and Review the risk with the stakeholders and explore simplifying the requirements In this scenario, the risk is not yet materialized so it is not an issue. It is important that the project manager address the possible impacts with stakeholders before taking action to make any change to the definition of the project strategy or approach implementation.

Questions.Text: In an environment subject to various elements of ambiguity and change, what should an agile project manager do to keep track of artifacts? (Choose two)
Options: Update the artifacts frequently and quickly in a transparent fashion.
Define which technology to use in the project management plan.
Define which technology to use in the communications management plan.
Increase the use of social computing tools.
Use historical information from previous projects as a template.
Questions.Explanation: Update the artifacts frequently and quickly in a transparent fashion and Define which technology to use in the communications management plan An agile project manager should keep track of artifacts by updating them in a frequent and transparent manner and defining communication technology that is available to develop, record, transmit, retrieve, track, and store communication artifacts.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working on a project charter and discovers that some of the key stakeholders in this project are moving to other departments and their replacements have not been identified yet. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Put the project on hold until the replacements are identified.
Obtain support from the project sponsor to continue the project.
Perform an impact analysis of the possible delays.
Involve senior management to resolve the challenge.
Questions.Explanation: Involve senior management to resolve the challenge The project manager should involve senior management because they have the power to resolve staffing-related challenges.

Questions.Text: A project manager has been assigned to an important project with stakeholders who have different expectations for the project. Some stakeholders also have a high degree of influence on the project. How should the project manager handle this situation?
Options: Control the degree of stakeholder influence to better align with the project objectives.
Include the influential stakeholders' requirements and ensure other stakeholders are aligned with the project goals.
Elicit and negotiate stakeholder requirements according to each stakeholder's influence.
Manage the stakeholders carefully and apply stakeholder expectations without impacting the project objectives.
Questions.Explanation: Elicit and negotiate stakeholder requirements according to each stakeholder's influence The project manager will first need to understand each stakeholder's influence and interest in the project thoroughly. Based on these two factors, the project manager can then negotiate and align each stakeholder's needs with the project goals. Thus, eliciting and negotiating stakeholder requirements according to each stakeholder's influence is the correct answer. The other answer choices are incorrect. It is not possible for the project manager to control the degree of stakeholder influence; or include influential stakeholder's request and make other stakeholders align with the project goals; or be able to manage stakeholder expectations and apply their expectations without accessing each one of the stakeholder's influence in the project.

Questions.Text: At the beginning of a project, the project manager meets the product owner and the business analyst to work on a requirement gathering process to create the list of requirements, features, and product backlog. Which elements should be considered to assure all user stories are well-written and sized correctly to allow a smooth agile project delivery? (Choose two)
Options: Independent, negotiable, and estimable
Unique, applicable, and replicable
Sub divisible, suitable, and considerable
Concise, detailed, and executable
Valuable, small, and testable
Questions.Explanation: Independent, negotiable, and estimable and Valuable, small, and testable In order to create good user stories, start by remembering to INVEST in good user stories. INVEST is an acronym which encompasses the following concepts which make up a good user story: •Independent - stories should be as independent as possible. •Negotiable - a story is not a contract. A story is an invitation to a conversation. •Valuable - if a story does not have discernable value it should not be done. •Estimable - a story has to be able to be estimated or sized so it can be properly prioritized. •Small - stories should be small chunks of work (eg.two week iterations average 3-4 days of work) •Testable - every story needs to be testable in order to be "done".

Questions.Text: A new team member is taking over the tasks for a team member who is going on leave. During the transition, the new team member thinks that several parts of the new application cannot be tested yet, despite sign-off by the outgoing team member. What should the project manager do to address this concern?
Options: Ensure the team member going on leave creates a knowledge transfer document for the team member taking over the tasks, highlighting the solution to their concerns.
Ask the team member going on leave to share the project charter, SOW, and quality assurance plans to demonstrate how they align.
Ask the new team member to complete a training video for this product.
Check the project readiness and direct the new team member to the shared project folder with the latest documentation.
Questions.Explanation: Check the project readiness and direct the new team member to the shared project folder with the latest documentation Plan Quality Management is the process of identifying quality requirements and/or standards for the project and its deliverables, and documenting how the project will demonstrate compliance with quality requirements and/or standards. The key benefit of this process is that it provides guidance and direction on how quality will be managed and verified throughout the project.

Questions.Text: The research and development team is planning to build a new facility that will focus on improving existing products that the organization has developed. It will be a multiyear initiative. What should the project manager do to plan for this?
Options: Develop a scope management plan that will focus on expected objectives of the initiative.
Develop a business case document that covers the return on investment (ROI) of the initiative.
Develop a resource management plan to cover resources and contingency planning.
Develop a communications management plan to address stakeholder needs.
Questions.Explanation: Develop a resource management plan to cover resources and contingency planning Before initiating any initiative, it is vital for the project manager to understand and estimate required resources that will be utilized during the delivery of the initiative

Questions.Text: During project execution, a key stakeholder is replaced with a new team member who has approval authority. The new member states that the project deliverables are not aligned with the design and does not approve them. However, a few weeks later the project manager learns that the new team member had not been informed of changes during execution. What should the project manager have done to avoid this miscommunication?
Options: Organized a meeting with the sponsor before execution began
Updated the stakeholder register to include the new team member
Asked the previous stakeholder to hold a meeting with the new team member before leaving
Updated the project charter during the execution phase to include all the changes
Questions.Explanation: Updated the project charter during the execution phase to include all the changes "Updated the project charter during the execution phase to include all the changes" is the best choice as managing the flow of information by following the up to date project documents in order to keep stakeholders engaged and informed. If a new stakeholder and/or scope changes, PM need to update the appropriate documents to reflect these changes "Organize a meeting with..." is not the right choice to avoid the miscommunication. The basis for communication is to have the updated list of stakeholders "Updated the stakeholder register..." is also a necessary step to be taken however updating the charter to reflect the changes is the best choice "Ask the previous stakeholder..." could be one of the activity for the PM, but this is not the best action compared to the updates to the project documents This question and rationale were developed in reference to: Agile Foundations - Principles, practices and frameworks (No Date) Peter Measey//13/ [Item] | PMBOK Guide Sixth Edition (2018) PMI/PMI/10. Project Communication Management/ [Item Communications management plan. Described in Section 10.1.3.1. The communications management plan is updated with new information to make communication more effective. Stakeholder engagement plan. Described in Section 13.2.3.1. The stakeholder engagement plan is updated to reflect the actual situation of stakeholders, their communication needs, and their importance.]

Questions.Text: The project scope statement is approved by the project sponsor. The team members would like to modify the work breakdown structure (WBS) without changing the scope and send a request to the project manager. What should the project manager do with this request?
Options: Determine which changes to implement and inform the team.
Decline the request and escalate it to the project sponsor.
Discuss it with the team members and make a decision by consensus.
Empower the team to make the decisions, then inform the sponsor.
Questions.Explanation: Empower the team to make the decisions, then inform the sponsor The project manager should empower the team to make the decisions so that the team owns the accountability of their accomplishments and understand their level of authority. The other answer choices are incorrect. Determining which changes to implement is not the best action as the empowering aspect is missing. Declining the request and escalate is not the right action as it shows the non-collaborative approach by a project manager. Discussing it with the team members is not the right choice as decisions should be based on facts and figures not by consensus.

Questions.Text: The business manager and the product owner are requesting a more flexible way to incorporate changes into the product backlog. They are concerned about losing time to market with respect to other vendors. What should the project manager do?
Options: Coach the product owner to prioritize product backlog items in accordance to business goals.
Review the assumption log and project management plan for changes.
Establish a Change Control Board (CCB) with key stakeholders.
Allow key stakeholders to make some changes to deliver the product faster.
Questions.Explanation: Coach the product owner to prioritize product backlog items in accordance to business goals. The prioritization of the backlog items is the responsibility of the product owner. The key is establishing the more adequate baseline for the selected methodology to initiate, review, approve or reject change requests.

Questions.Text: A project manager is assigned to a new project and begins building the team. The project manager quickly discovers that some of the team members are concerned with their personal development, whereas others lack the technical skills required to perform the necessary work. What should the project manager do first?
Options: Work with the functional manager of each team area to provide substitute team members who better align with project objectives.
Analyze the skills of each team member against the project requirements to identify gaps and develop action plans to fulfill the gaps.
Schedule a team workshop with the customer to demonstrate the product objectives.
Initiate the first sprint and let the team self-organize to solve the gaps that they identify.
Questions.Explanation: Analyze the skills of each team member against the project requirements to identify gaps and develop action plans to fulfill the gaps. The project manager develops a team aligning individual objectives of team members with the project objectives and manages the gaps for effective collaboration. Therefore, the first step would be to analyze the skills of each team member and identify gaps before taking action.

Questions.Text: A project manager is not sure if the team has a critical skill needed to implement a key feature of an upcoming deliverable. In order to mitigate the risk of sprint failure, what should the project manager do?
Options: Ask for support from the sponsor to replace the low performers on the team with new team members who have the specific skill set.
Negotiate with the stakeholder to postpone this key feature to a later release while figuring out a better alternative.
Use the next retrospective to assess the skill gap and propose a strategy for training or scope outsourcing.
Embrace the risk and accept the failure, if it happens, and motivate the team toward better performance in the future.
Questions.Explanation: Use the next retrospective to assess the skill gap and propose a strategy for training or scope outsourcing. In order to mitigate the risk of sprint failure, the project manager should use the next retrospective to assess the skill gap and propose a strategy for training or scope outsourcing. Retrospectives help to identify previous experiences. The project manager can decide on mitigation or transfer once they identify the requirements for measuring team capabilities.

Questions.Text: A project is estimated to be a total of 250 story points. The team planned 40 story points for each iteration and has delivered 40, 10, 20, and 15 story points in the first four iterations. The project manager realizes that the team will not be able to deliver all of the features. What should the project manager do?
Options: Reassign undelivered tasks to team members to complete during the next iteration.
Work with team members to adjust the planned story points for the next iterations.
Remove the features that cannot be delivered from the product backlog and update the customer.
Negotiate with the customer for an extension of the deliverables due to holiday schedules.
Questions.Explanation: Work with team members to adjust the planned story points for the next iterations. The project manager should work with team members to adjust the planned story points for the next iterations. Based on the four iterations the team's average is 21 story points per iteration but the team planned 40 story points for each iteration. Hence, the project manager needs to be practical in planning the story points per iteration. The other answer choices are incorrect. Reassigning undelivered tasks to team members to complete during the next iteration is wrong because the project manager does not assign tasks, the team is a self-organizing team. Removing the features that cannot be delivered from the product backlog and updating the customer is wrong because the project manager does not remove features from the backlog. Negotiating with the customer for an extension of the deliverables due to holiday schedules is wrong.

Questions.Text: An agile team is receiving multiple unplanned requests for product enhancements in an iteration. This impediment is causing team to slip scheduled tasks. How should project manager/scrum master solve this impediment?
Options: Request team to spend additional effort to accommodate enhancements.
Ask product owner to include enhancements in current iteration.
Request team to reprioritize committed activities.
Ask product owner to add requests to product backlog.
Questions.Explanation: Ask product owner to add requests to product backlog. Agile projects welcome changes to improve the product during project execution but in a proper manner. Asking the product owner to start a backlog refinement in order to include enhancements will allow reviewing product backlog with team and reprioritize remaining work. Additional efforts to accommodate enhancements to current iteration should not be encouraged.

Questions.Text: A strategic project has been launched and a project manager has been assigned. As the project is progressing and the team is delivering successfully, a key stakeholder calls the project manager and complains about not receiving progress updates. What should the project manager do first?
Options: Ensure the key stakeholder is copied on the weekly project status emails.
Review and update the project communications management plan.
Send a copy of the latest project status report to the key stakeholder.
Invite the key stakeholder to the next project meeting.
Questions.Explanation: Review and update the project communications management plan. The project manager should identify all stakeholders and ensure the communications plan is up to date. The Project Communications Plan should include the method, frequency and level of details for all stakeholders. As per PMBOK: «There are two parts to successful communication. The first part involves developing an appropriate communication strategy based on both the needs of the project and the project’s stakeholders. From that strategy, a communications management plan is developed to ensure that the appropriate messages are communicated to stakeholders in various formats and various means as defined by the communication strategy" PMBoK Sixth Edition in Chapter 10 states: "PROJECT COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT Project Communications Management includes the processes necessary to ensure that the information needs of the project and its stakeholders are met through development of artifacts and implementation of activities designed to achieve effective information exchange. Project Communications Management consists of two parts. The first part is developing a strategy to ensure communication is effective for stakeholders. The second part is carrying out the activities necessary to implement the communication strategy. The Project Communications Management processes are: 10.1 Plan Communications Management—The process of developing an appropriate approach and plan for project communication activities based on the information needs of each stakeholder or group, available organizational assets, and the needs of the project. 10.2 Manage Communications—The process of ensuring timely and appropriate collection, creation, distribution, storage, retrieval, management, monitoring, and the ultimate disposition of project information. 10.3 Monitor Communications—the process of ensuring the information needs of the project and its stakeholders are met" The first activity the project manager needs, therefore, to do is to: «Review and update the project communications management plan" The Distractors are clearly worded are valid and accepted

Questions.Text: A company is about to start a project in a remote place known for extreme weather. It was agreed to implement the project using a predicted, multiphase approach. Each phase is composed of several iterations. What should the project manager do in the event of extreme weather?
Options: Pause work until a specialized team arrives to assess the damage that was done to the site.
Consult the initial plans in case of an emergency, then update and follow the corrective procedures.
Advise management to reconsider such an investment and the continuity of the project.
Stop the iteration, run a retrospective, and move to the next phase of the project.
Questions.Explanation: Consult the initial plans in case of an emergency, then update and follow the corrective procedures. The correct answer is to consult the initial plans in case of an emergency, then update and follow the corrective procedures. Planning risk responses is the process of developing options, selecting strategies, and agreeing on actions to address overall project risk exposure, as well as to treat individual project risks. The other options are incorrect because the first thing that the project manager should do is consult the initial plans. The risk of extreme weather was acknowledged. Pausing work or stopping the iteration may turn out to be the approved risk response, but the project manager must consult the initial plan to determine that. Advising management to reconsider is not an appropriate course of action because those discussions should have occurred prior to starting the project.

Questions.Text: A project manager has been assigned a project that involves a virtual, multicultural team including representatives from various countries. The project manager needs to prepare a kickoff meeting. What should the project manager do to prepare for the meeting?
Options: Set rules and regulations that must be followed during the project.
Ask the sponsor for different resources to facilitate the project.
Consider various time zones, communication capabilities, and possible constraints.
Set up the project sponsor's language as the official project communication language.
Questions.Explanation: Consider various time zones, communication capabilities, and possible constraints. The project manager has to consider various internal and external enterprise environmental factors (EEFs) related to variables that affect, restrain, or influence the project team. This includes geographically spread virtual teams with multicultural backgrounds in different time zones, it's crucial for the PM to understand the team's culture, availability, and preferences for the kickoff meetings.

Questions.Text: A project manager used to work on predictive projects and now has started working on a project using a hybrid approach. What should the project manager do to enable the teams to focus on their mission?
Options: Implement a Scrum method.
Adopt servant leadership.
Apply lean methods.
Innovate with new processes.
Questions.Explanation: Adopt servant leadership. Servant leader exhibits a commitment to serve and prioritize teams tasks; growth, learning, development, autonomy, and well-being, He prioritizes connections, community, and cooperation; leadership is secondary and develops after service. PM moving to a different project should adopt and exhibit Servant Leadership skills before directing the team.

Questions.Text: A project is in a key phase of execution. Everyone is dedicated to achieving success on the project. A functional manager suddenly takes away one of the key team members to respond to an emergency without communicating this to the project manager. What should the project manager do?
Options: Check the resource calendar to find another team member.
Report the situation to the project sponsor and executive management.
Communicate with the functional manager and request the return of the key member.
Review the impact to the project and schedule a meeting with the functional manager.
Questions.Explanation: Review the impact to the project and schedule a meeting with the functional manager. The Project Manager needs to evaluate the impact and discuss this directly with the Functional Manager of the consequence. Hence this is the correct option. Since the Functional manager had taken one of the key team member suddenly, this meant that the functional manager would no way return the team member. Hence the choice of requesting the functional manager to return the resource does not arise. Hence this option is incorrect. Finding another team member or reporting the situation to the project sponsor and executive management will not solve the current issue. Hence these two options are incorrect.

Questions.Text: A senior team member was very influential and played a crucial role in past projects. In this new project, the senior member no longer plays a crucial role and is demotivated. What should the project manager do to address this situation?
Options: Arrange a team meeting to discuss ways of increasing motivation.
Provide incentives to increase motivation.
Ask the functional manager to speak with the team member.
Arrange a one-on-one meeting with the team member.
Questions.Explanation: Arrange a one-on-one meeting with the team member. Feedback sessions are usually the best option to assess team members' concerns and build a relationship to increase motivation. The project manager performs communication roles with team members. The project manager often uses soft skills like interpersonal skills and the ability to manage people within informal networks. These informal networks that project managers develop, maintain, and nurture are more important than formal org structures. Informal networks include the use of established relationships with individuals such as subject matter experts and influential leaders.

Questions.Text: During product development, two functional departments indicate that they are short on resources and cannot build and deliver all the products on time. What should the project manager do to address this concern?
Options: Escalate the issue to the sponsor and request confirmation of the project scope.
Ask the functional manager to hire additional resources to meet the requirements.
Request team members work extra hours to meet the commercial needs.
Analyze the critical path to deliver the minimum viable product (MVP).
Questions.Explanation: Analyze the critical path to deliver the minimum viable product (MVP). A concept that projects can use when constrained is the Minimum Viable Product (mvp), which is a scope management paradigm. When scope is too large and time/budgets are limited, we identify the MVP to deliver an acceptable outcome. The PM should have already confirmed the project scope with the sponsor, secured appropriate resources, and allocated sufficient hours.

Questions.Text: Members of an agile team are struggling. While there is trust among the team members, the majority of team decisions are made by a few of the more experienced team members. What should the project manager do in order to enable a high-performing team?
Options: Discourage the vocal team members from speaking first, allowing others to offer ideas.
Involve management to coach all team members to take part in team decisions.
Coach the team to ensure all members offer ideas and team consensus is achieved.
Raise the issue in a retrospective and have the team formalize new team agreements.
Questions.Explanation: Coach the team to ensure all members offer ideas and team consensus is achieved. Hands-on agile coaching can greatly speed your adoption and increase your chance of success. Infusing experienced agile professionals who take on key roles such as team lead can be helpful. Pairing of team members with different skill levels can also help increase the effectiveness of your team.

Questions.Text: The project manager of a large team notices team members are not interacting together, which is impacting project performance and the quality of the project outcome. What should the project manager do to help grow team dynamics?
Options: Conduct face-to-face group meetings to understand what the issues are.
Notify the project team members' supervisors about the lack of cooperation.
Add the lack of team communication as a risk in the risk register and monitor the situation.
Refer to the lessons learned to understand how other projects handled similar issues.
Questions.Explanation: Conduct face-to-face group meetings to understand what the issues are. "Conduct face-to-face group meetings to understand what the issues are" is the correct choice as, to have effective results and improve the customer satisfaction, project manager should work continuously on nurturing team members, by listening to their comments and giving feedback regularly. This will help understand what's going on Refer to the lessons learned..." appears to be an option but this will not give a solution to the situation as the context is different. "Add the lack of team communication..." could be one of the steps the Project Manager would be doing but this not going to help grow the team members. "Notify the project..." is not going to help improve the situation

Questions.Text: The construction of a building is well-defined through plans and written specifications. The service is to be delivered in two years in a high-inflation environment. What contract type should the project manager use?
Options: Cost plus incentive fee (CPIF) contract
Firm fixed price (FFP) contract
Fixed price with economic price adjustment (FPEPA) contract
Time and materials (T&M) contract
Questions.Explanation: Fixed price with economic price adjustment (FPEPA) contract The project manager should use the fixed price with economic price adjustments (FPEPA) contract. This type is used whenever the seller's performance period spans a considerable period of years, or if the payments are made in a different currency. It is a fixed-price contract, but with a special provision allowing for predefined final adjustments to the contract price due to changed conditions, such as inflation changes or cost increases (or decreases) for specific commodities.

Questions.Text: A project manager received a complaint that the project team did not respond correctly to a client's request. However, the team members understand that the team has received multiple out-of-scope requests from the client. Which action should the project manager take?
Options: Encourage the team members to ask the "five whys" during their communications with the client.
Ask the client to better explain the issues and be more clear.
Review the scope with the client so that additional requests can be documented and processed as change requests.
Request to be included in all future communications between the project team members and the client.
Questions.Explanation: Review the scope with the client so that additional requests can be documented and processed as change requests. Defining the scope is critical to successful project completion. Changes following scope approval are subject to change control procedures. Asking the five whys, may or may not resolve the issue It is assumed that the has been clear with their communication Proceeding without following the change control process may be a risk to successful project completion.

Questions.Text: The development of a project requires incremental delivery of the product. Project delivery involves the efforts of both local and remote team members. What communication strategy should be used in this situation?
Options: Implement the communications plan to ensure communications relative to project delivery are performed in accordance with the plan.
Ensure each country team sends detailed status reports to the project manager
Schedule one-on-one biweekly calls with each country team
Send daily email updates between each of the country teams
Questions.Explanation: Implement the communications plan to ensure communications relative to project delivery are performed in accordance with the plan. The communications plan should identify the approach to team communications so that issues if they occur can be immediately addressed. Detailed status reports, one-on one biweekly calls and daily e mails may not provide the communication necessary to address the issues.

Questions.Text: A project team using a hybrid approach is producing both predictive and incremental deliverables simultaneously. The communications management plan states that written communications are preferred over other types. Problems arise daily regarding the incremental tasks; however, the email backlog is becoming increasingly and unnecessarily complex. What should the project manager do?
Options: Adopt face-to-face conversations during sprints.
Adopt judicial communications during sprints.
Adopt disruptive communications during sprints.
Adopt formal conversations during sprints.
Questions.Explanation: Adopt face-to-face conversations during sprints. One of the principles of Agile is to prioritize "face-to-face conversations". The other answer choices are incorrect. Although the project has a waterfall component and the Communications plan states written communications are preferred, in what concerns to the agile part, the PM as a servant leader must eliminate organizational hurdles and unnecessary complexity, at least to the Agile portion

Questions.Text: In a hybrid project, the need for customer involvement throughout the project is not understood by the customer. As a consequence, rework is beyond acceptable levels. What should the project manager do to address this issue?
Options: Submit a change request to obtain the required involvement from the customer.
Add this customer issue to the backlog for prioritization and review.
Meet with the customer to highlight the importance of their engagement for project success.
Instruct senior management to make the customer work closely with the team.
Questions.Explanation: Meet with the customer to highlight the importance of their engagement for project success. The first action would be to directly engage the customer to gain commitment for their involvement, which is key for success of a hybrid project. Such involvement does not require a change request, and it is premature to approach senior management. Adding the issue to the backlog does not solve the problem.

Questions.Text: When reviewing the project team's performance, the project manager realizes that the team velocity is not fast enough to meet the next review gate goal. What should the project manager do?
Options: Prioritize the contents of the backlog and remove some stories.
Increase the sprint duration to allow the team more time.
Use the standup meeting to assess impediments and problems.
Create a change request to modify the review gate goals.
Questions.Explanation: Use the standup meeting to assess impediments and problems. The project is being executed in a hybrid environment, overall project execution is assessed during gate reviews (traditional) but at the team level agile techniques are being used (Kanban board, daily stand ups). The daily stand up is the appropriate technique available for the project manager to continuously assess problems and impediments. The other answer choices are incorrect. The project manager does not prioritize the backlog, the product owner does. Increasing sprint duration is not an appropriate and advisable solution that could lead to long term planning issues. Creating a change request is also not a viable solution in this case.

Questions.Text: At the first meeting about a road construction project, the project manager reminds the team of the importance of observing all rules when carrying out their duties and interacting with stakeholders. Later in the project, team members start coming to meetings late and do not follow other ground rules. To ensure the project outcomes and schedule are not affected, what should the project manager do next?
Options: Address the issue with the specific team members who violated the ground rules.
Remove the team members who violated the ground rules.
Promote all team members who do not violate any ground rules.
Allow the team to investigate any team members who violate the ground rules.
Questions.Explanation: Address the issue with the specific team members who violated the ground rules. Conflict should be addressed first and usually in private, using a direct, collaborative approach. The other answer choices are incorrect. They do not promote a healthy team spirit. If disruptive conflict continues, formal procedures may be used, including disciplinary actions.

Questions.Text: Due to the complex nature of a predictive project, an organization decides to use an agile approach for a project phase. A team member does not really understand the agile approach and keeps allocating tasks to the rest of the team members. After a while, the team is not able to adjust by itself and conflicts arise, delaying the deliverables. What should the project manager do?
Options: Replace the team member with someone who has agile experience.
Wait until the team is finally able to solve the conflict by itself and reorganize.
Reaffirm the principles and roles of agile to create structures that the team can use.
Take control of the sprint backlog and assign tasks to the team members.
Questions.Explanation: Reaffirm the principles and roles of agile to create structures that the team can use. The project manager should reaffirm the principles and roles of agile to create structures that the team can use. When agile values and ground rules are understood and applied, The other answer choices are incorrect. The project manager should try to adjust behaviors and align on values, roles and responsibilities before considering replacing a team member. Waiting for the team is wrong because some time already passed and the team is still on the same page and some delays are occurring. The project manager does not own the backlog and most likely does not understands all user stories and tasks. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: Coaching Agile Teams (00/00/0000) Lyssa Adkins//Chapter 9. Coach as Conflict Navigator/WHAT SHOULD YOU DO ABOUT IT? Use Structures [Item Use Structures One step up on the power curve of response modes entails using the “bones” of agile to navigate conflict. The bones are those principles, values, or roles that help the team understand how to get the best from using agile.

Questions.Text: A multiyear project may be impacted by the imminent retirement of a key resource. The project manager is concerned about the impact on the project. What should the project manager do?
Options: Require the team members to work additional hours to make up for any unassigned tasks.
Advise the stakeholders that the key resource's tasks will not be completed after their departure.
Work with the resource manager to identify and train a replacement.
Encourage the team members to volunteer for the key resource's position.
Questions.Explanation: Work with the resource manager to identify and train a replacement. For all project teams, training requirements should be identified. Back up competencies should be required for the team. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: Project Management Essentials, 2nd Edition (No Date) Kathryn N. Wells and Professor Emeritus Timothy J. Kloppenborg//Chapter 3/ [Item One issue faced by many project teams is the fact that the project manager has limited direct control over the other team members.]

Questions.Text: A project manager who is handling multiple projects in five different countries has issued a new directive on workplace attire and office arrangements. This has been met with resistance in three countries, resulting in a series of misunderstandings with the project manager. What should the project manager have done?
Options: Contacted senior management before issuing a general directive
Assessed the cultural and personal traits in the various countries before issuing a general directive
Determined the socioeconomic status of the team members before issuing a general directive
Obtained a list of preferences from the team members before issuing a general directive
Questions.Explanation: Assessed the cultural and personal traits in the various countries before issuing a general directive the PM must take into consideration the behavioral, cultural and personality traits of all team members in order to deal with in an emotionally intelligent manner.

Questions.Text: A construction project has progressed for about 18 months, and the schedule performance index (SPI) is 0.68. The project manager intends to complete the project as planned without additional cost. What should the project manager do to complete the project on time?
Options: Crash the schedule.
Fast track the schedule.
Reduce the project scope.
Reduce the project quality.
Questions.Explanation: Fast track the schedule. Fast tracking provides opportunity to recover without any additional cost, but it might add some risks to be considered in the project. The other answer choices are incorrect. Crashing the schedule will incur additional cost; changes to project scope and quality must go through the change management process.

Questions.Text: A project manager works for a company that is involved in complex turnkey projects. During a close assessment of the team, the project manager finds that the team does not have experience in adjusting to frequent changes with project requirements and communications. What should the project manager do to ensure great team performance?
Options: Let the sponsor communicate with the team to improve performance.
Communicate with stakeholders regularly to avoid frequent changes.
Provide training to the team on using a hybrid approach.
Encourage more communications with the team.
Questions.Explanation: Provide training to the team on using a hybrid approach. Little experience on highbred approach is very critical, hence, addressing the change on methodology with the team by capacity building will be the first to yield results out of the listed impediments

Questions.Text: A project manager observes that team members are not collaborating or being proactive. What should the project manager have done at the beginning of the project to achieve a better project environment?
Options: Conducted an assessment on team dynamics.
Assigned all tasks at the beginning of the project.
Held additional team meetings.
Split the project into more iterations.
Questions.Explanation: conducted an assessment on team dynamics. It is critical that the project manager have a good grasp of the corporate culture at the beginning of the project. If a culture survey had been conducted the project manager would have known that the culture of the company was not a proactive one, and they could have found ways to address the problem

Questions.Text: A project manager working in an agile environment realizes that some new team members have difficulty understanding what older team members did to get certain results on the project. What should the project manager do?
Options: Organize a stakeholder meeting.
Ensure team members focus on their tasks.
Assess the need for knowledge transfer.
Encourage and facilitate peer mentoring.
Questions.Explanation: Encourage and facilitate peer mentoring. Peer mentoring is a priority for project continuation so the project manager needs to discuss project responsibilities within the team and ensure that all of the silo mentors are identified and have been asked to share their knowledge to others on the project team. They also want to ensure that every new project or team member is assigned a primary mentor to ease their introduction to the team and reduce the sort of fumbling around that comes with new help. The other answer choices are incorrect. Organizing a stakeholder meeting and ensuring team member’s focus on their tasks does not address the need for shared knowledge; the need for assessing knowledge transfer has already been established. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: O'Reilly Platform (No Date) //Chapter 1. Roles in Peer mentoring/ [Item Teach What You Know: A Practical Leader’s Guide to Knowledge Transfer Using Peer Mentoring by Steve Trautman Published by Prentice Hall, 2006 CAST OF CHARACTERS IN PEER MENTORING I’m going to introduce you to four different players who have a big role in this book. Peer Mentors One way to help clarify the job is to separate out two different ways people transfer knowledge: •Primary Peer Mentors. Regularly called upon to transfer knowledge, primary peer mentors often orient new hires or take on other long-term relationships, such as cross training a person to a new position after reorganization. Primary peer mentors have an “umbrella” of knowledge and responsibilities. They look after their apprentices’ big-picture needs, answer general questions, and teach specific skills. They’re on deck for everything, from soup to nuts. •Silo Peer Mentors. Subject matter experts with a deep “silo” of knowledge, the silo peer mentors are generally better suited to teaching their specialty and not as well suited to worrying about apprentices beyond that. Some silo mentors are people who are expected to establish, teach, and monitor a standard practice among existing employees. Others are responsible for keeping an eye on quality, especially when the manager isn’t a subject matter expert. It is common for managers to be concerned about the silos of information that exist in their organizations, because there are many problems that arise when information or skills are too narrowly held. One solution is to identify the silo mentors and then redefine their roles to include outreach and training of others. Including such sharing of knowledge in their roles means they are no longer the only ones who know a specific concept or skill. If you think your organization would benefit from mining the knowledge of silo mentors, you should bring it up with your manager. Manager Managers include supervisors, project managers, team leads, and others who use peer mentoring tools and ideas to improve the overall communication and management of their human resources. They want to ensure that all of the silo mentors are identified and have been asked to transfer their knowledge to others on the project team. They also want to ensure that every new project or team member is assigned a primary mentor to ease their introduction to the team and reduce the sort of fumbling around that comes with new help.]

Questions.Text: A project manager realizes that the team needs training on a specific technology that is required in the project. What should the project manager do to mitigate this risk?
Options: Assess the team's knowledge on the technology and look for recommended training options.
Contract with an expert provider to do the piece of work that uses that specific technology.
Create an item in the risk log and notify the sponsor.
Fix any training gaps as they arise during the project.
Questions.Explanation: Assess the team's knowledge on the technology and look for recommended training options. The project manager needs to know their team skills in order to assign them accordingly. If the team is lacking knowledge on the technology, the project manager must assess the risk look for recommended training options. The other answer choices are incorrect. It cannot be assumed that there is time and funds to contract the expert provider to use the technology; creating an item in the risk log identifies the risk but does not mitigate it; and fixing the training gaps without assessing where the gaps are in the team knowledge is not good practice. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: Mastering Project Human Resource Management (No Date) Harjit Singh//6/ [Item Improving project team skills and competencies By comparing the wanted (target) skills and competencies with current (baseline) skills and competencies By offering training and development opportunities to fill the skills and competencies gaps]

Questions.Text: A project manager is assigned to a long-term project with a duration of three years. Which approach should the project manager take to address near-term activities, while long-term work is planned at a high level and detailed later?
Options: Focus on acquiring a team and deliver as soon as possible.
Engage the sponsor in the validation and prioritization processes.
Perform workshops with the client for delivery dates.
Validate the requirements and perform rolling wave planning.
Questions.Explanation: Validate the requirements and perform rolling wave planning. As we progress through the items on our road-map, the feedback we gather as we begin to validate our ideas at each step will inform what we need to do in the future. With a Rolling Wave Plan, we should have regular checkpoints to incorporate new information and adjust our plan accordingly. The other answer choices are incorrect. They do not address the need for the progressive elaboration required in a long-term project. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: O'Reilly Platform (No Date) //Managing Technology-Based Projects: Tools, Techniques, People and Business Processes by Hans J. Thamhain/Glossary [Item Rolling wave planning Progressive planning, providing more detail for near-term activities, while long-term work is planned at a high level and detailed later.]

Questions.Text: A company in the financial industry is kicking off a new hybrid project to be in compliance with new government regulations. The team has established a reliable velocity of 65 story points per iteration. Every iteration lasts 2 weeks. The sponsor wants to know when the project will be done if there are 1,040 story points remaining. How many months will it take to complete the project?
Options: 2 months
4 months
8 months
16 months
Questions.Explanation: 8 months There are 1040 story points remaining, velocity = 65 points per iteration. # of iterations remaining = 1040 / 65 = 16. Every iteration lasts 2 weeks, then the team will need 8 months to complete the project.

Questions.Text: A company is kicking off a new hybrid project to be in compliance with government regulations. The team has established a velocity of 50 story points per iteration. Every iteration lasts 1 week. The sponsor wants to know when the project will be done if there are 1,500 story points remaining.
Options: 30 weeks
15 weeks
4 weeks
1 week
Questions.Explanation: 30 weeks There are 1,500 story points remaining, velocity = 50 points per iteration. # of iterations remaining = 1,500/ 50 = 30. Every iteration lasts 1 week, then the team will need 30 weeks to complete the project.

Questions.Text: A scrum team is using a hybrid approach with 2-week sprints. The minimum viable product (MVP) release is at the end of the current sprint, and the team has completed the must and should features; however, some of the other priority features are still in development. The project manager is preparing the monthly report for the steering committee. What should the project manager recommend?
Options: Reschedule the MVP release review until all the agreed-on features have been completed.
Update the project management plan to include in the release only the features completed.
Organize the sprint review and proceed with releasing the MVP only with the features completed.
Estimate the effort required and submit a change request to add more resources to the team.
Questions.Explanation: Organize the sprint review and proceed with releasing the MVP only with the features completed. The purpose of the MVP is to gather feedback. If the core functionality is ready the release should proceed as planned.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working on a new project. Two key project resources have a disagreement regarding a requirement. The project manager wants to understand both points of view, but one resource is not willing to meet with the project manager. What should the project manager do?
Options: Escalate the issue to the project sponsor.
Accept the position of the senior project resource.
Review the organizational process assets (OPAs).
Refer to the team's ground rules and project vision
Questions.Explanation: Refer to the team's ground rules and project vision. The project manager must obtain an agreement on how the team should proceed, and this can be found in the established ground rules. They must also obtain consensus and use this information to resolve conflicts in alignment with the project vision. The other answer choices are incorrect. Escalating the issue without first trying to resolve it is not good practice; accepting the position of the senior project resource is not good conflict resolution; the OPAs are higher-level and do not necessarily address specific team resolutions.

Questions.Text: A new project manager is assigned to a project in the execution phase. The project just completed the deliverables for the first phase. During a workshop to review the scope for the second phase of the project, a couple of stakeholders complain that they were not informed of the benefits already delivered. What should the project manager do to resolve this issue?
Options: Escalate the issue to the steering committee because the sponsor should have informed stakeholders.
Escalate the issue to the sponsor because the question came from some of the senior stakeholders.
Record the issue in the issue log, discuss it with the change manager, and update the communications management plan.
Record the issue in the register, review the stakeholder matrix, and update the risk management plan.
Questions.Explanation: Record the issue in the issue log, discuss it with the change manager, and update the communications management plan. Stakeholders that are not aware of the benefits and the value delivered throughout the project is a common issue and it is the responsibility of the project manager to resolve it when it happens. The root cause is poor stakeholder assessment and/or communication plan. Communication is the responsibility of the project manager, sometimes involving the change manager. The other answer choices are incorrect. Escalating to the steering committee and sponsor is incorrect because it is not their responsibility to resolve the issue. The PM has access to the stakeholders because they were invited to the workshop. Updating the risk management plan is not helpful here because this is an issue that has already occurred, not a risk.

Questions.Text: A project is close to completion, and the customer wants to celebrate the success by sharing a business case about the benefits created by the project. What should the project manager add to their benefits report to the customer?
Options: Improved project schedule
Return on investment (ROI)
Risks identified during the project
Successful change requests
Questions.Explanation: Return on investment (ROI) Opt for the direct and measurable benefits to compare against the cost of doing the project and the cost of operating the new process. If the benefits outweigh the costs over the expected life of the project deliverables, senior management may be willing to support the project.

Questions.Text: A project manager working in a matrix organization is managing a project that is scheduled to be completed in one month. At today's project meeting, the project manager was informed that errors encountered in the software code are steadily increasing. During discussions with the development team lead, the project manager is told that this is normal when trying to stay on schedule. The development team is pressing to move on so a deadline is not missed, but the project manager is concerned. What should the project manager do?
Options: Update the work breakdown structure (WBS) and extend the durations in the project schedule to allow more development time.
Direct the team to continue development to avoid missing the deadline, noting that the risk has been mitigated.
Examine the quality metrics to determine if the project is complying with the documented quality requirements.
Submit a change request through the change control process to extend the deadline, allowing more time for development.
Questions.Explanation: Examine the quality metrics to determine if the project is complying with the documented quality requirements. Examine the quality: A Quality metric describes a project or product attribute & how the Control Quality process will verify compliance. The PM checks the metrics to see if the increase in, or the number of errors, is out of compliance. Until the check is done, it is not known if the increase is out compliance The other answer choices are incorrect. Continue development: The project manager may press the team to continue, but until the quality metric is checked, it may result in non-compliance. Update the WBS: The WBS would only be changed if there was to be a change in scope and there is no indication of that being the case. The schedule would have to be changed if there was an indication that additional time is needed and only after the change is approved Submit change request: The project manager should not submit a change request until there is something to change. At this stage there is no indication that additional time is needed to warrant such a request.

Questions.Text: An organization has a policy that project managers must carry out two audits per month; an audit for their own project and an audit for another project manager's project. After a recent audit review, team members suggest that it is not fair that their deliverables are being audited by a specific person. The project manager knows this is not true. What should the project manager do to avoid this situation in future projects?
Options: Complete audits less frequently.
Use casual meetings instead of audits.
Include the organization's audit policy in the scope management plan.
Ask the organization to advise all staff that audits are a requirement.
Questions.Explanation: Include the organization's audit policy in the scope management plan. Set up audit policy: Establishing the audit requirement in the project management plan is the way to bring transparency to the process. The Audit may be tailored and is the most appropriate tool to measure any potential gaps in progress reporting, risk, quality, scope etc. Audits less frequent: This doesn't address the concern raised by team members. Casual meetings in lieu of audits: An audit is a deliberate process. Casual meetings negate the reason for having an audit. Advising staff: This may be useful to ensure all are familiar with the process, however this would occur only after the audit policy is defined in the project management plan.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working with remote team members who are acting independently. What should the project manager do to promote collaboration among team members?
Options: Include the team members in all emails regarding the project.
Call team members individually to collect information and provide updates weekly.
Promote regular meetings using video conferencing.
Ask each team member to fill out a task log for daily updates.
Questions.Explanation: Promote regular meetings using video conferencing. The best option is to encourage regular meetings where all parties can connect and develop a sense of collaboration. The other options are not the most efficient way to operate and may not promote collaboration.

Questions.Text: A project manager has just been given a project team. The goal is to assign tasks to the team as quickly as possible so they can begin working on the project. Which approach should the project manager take to ensure the right tasks are assigned to the right resources?
Options: Distribute the tasks evenly since everyone on the team should have equal skill sets.
Assign the most complex tasks to the most experienced resources.
Evaluate the background, experience, and capabilities of the team, then assign tasks.
Assign the most challenging tasks to junior resources with support from experienced resources.
Questions.Explanation: Evaluate the background, experience, and capabilities of the team, then assign tasks. Once you know your team’s current knowledge, you can determine which is the most appropriate tasks to be assigned.

Questions.Text: During the project kick-off meeting, the project manager announces that a hybrid approach will be used to deliver the project. Many of the team members raise a concern because they are used to working on predictive projects. What should the project manager do?
Options: Escalate the issue to the project sponsor to ensure the team is aligned with the project delivery approach.
Engage the project management office (PMO) since they identify the approach to delivering projects.
Educate the team members on agile and the benefits associated with the use of agile principles.
Change to a predictive approach to be aligned with the skills of the team members.
Questions.Explanation: Educate the team members on agile and the benefits associated with the use of agile principles.
