Before changing the delivery approach the PM needs to work with the team to ensure an understanding of the benefits of agile Escalating to the sponsor or PMO or changing to the predictive approach are not the first steps a PM would do in this situation

Questions.Text: A project manager is assigned to a mobile application development project. The project manager realizes that an agile approach is better suited for the project, but the team is not skilled in agile approaches. What should the project manager do next if the organization is open to using any project management approach?
Options: Request that the project be assigned to an agile project team.
Ask to replace the team with members who have used agile approaches.
Arrange training on agile principles and mindset for the existing project team.
Use a predictive approach since the project team is already familiar with that approach.
Questions.Explanation: Arrange training on agile principles and mindset for the existing project team. As the project manager you are expected to build your team by continuously enhancing their skills to meet project needs

Questions.Text: An oil refinery project is in the middle of the execution phase. The project team found that the project is delayed because of an environmental regulation change, procurement delays, and a change in government. However, the project deliverable is still urgently needed. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Terminate the project immediately, avoid further loss through more expenditure, and absorb the current loss.
Review the impact of the changes, comply with the new regulations, then propose a realistic plan.
Make the necessary arrangements for the procurement materials, and continue the project as planned.
Stop the project and negotiate with the new government to pay the invoices for work done up to that point.
Questions.Explanation: Review the impact of the changes, comply with the new regulations, then propose a realistic plan Any regulatory requirements or changes to requirements must be reviewed and aligned with the new regulations.

Questions.Text: During execution, the customer submits a change request to maintain the project's business value, which has been threatened by a recently implemented government regulatory change. The project manager analyzes the change, which will require 50% of the project deliverable to be reworked and will incur in additional costs to the customer. What should the project manager do?
Options: Submit the change request to the CCB as the customer is absorbing the cost.
Submit the change request to the CCB and advise the panel to consider rejecting the request due to its impact.
Submit the change request to the CCB and explain the reason for the request, including a cost-impact analysis.
Submit the change request to show the change as having no impact, then proceed with the request without notifying the CCB.
Questions.Explanation: Submit the change request to the CCB and explain the reason for the request, including a cost-impact analysis The project manager should document and submit the change request to the CCB for review and approval. Since there is significant cost impact, the cost-impact analysis must be part of the submission. The other answer choices are incorrect. Whether the customer is absorbing the cost or not is irrelevant to the change request. A change request is to capture the reasons for change, the impact to the project, and other relevant facts. It is not required for the project manager to advice as part of the request. Project manager should follow code of ethics and must not make up or omit any important information from the CCB.

Questions.Text: During project closure, the team considers that the final sprint retrospective is not necessary because there are no remaining sprints. What could be a possible helpful outcome of holding this sprint retrospective?
Options: Improvement in velocity if the same team is formed in the future
Promotion opportunities for the team members
Information that could be useful for future projects
Tips for adapting to working together if that happens in the near future
Questions.Explanation: Information that could be useful for future projects Retrospective are a key practice during the project. Although the project is near the end the information could be useful for feeding lessons learned database of the organization or company for other teams or project.

Questions.Text: During a one-year project, a team delivered everything as requested and defined by the product owner and met all of the sprint goals. The project was on track regarding schedule, scope, and budget. A few months after the project release, the product satisfaction surveys show that the customer's level of satisfaction is surprisingly low. What should the project manager do to prevent this situation in the future?
Options: Hold a kick-off meeting at the beginning of the project.
Improve application of test automation techniques.
Increase project management office (PMO) involvement.
Conduct periodic reviews with customer involvement.
Questions.Explanation: Conduct periodic reviews with customer involvement The project manager should conduct periodic reviews with the customer. During the whole project, there was no real user involvement and input on the product being created. Although the team was performing well, they were not creating the 'right' product to satisfy customers. The other answer choices are incorrect. Holding a kick-off meeting at beginning of project is not sufficient; and increasing PMO involvement and test automation do not address customer satisfaction issue.

Questions.Text: A company is implementing Agile. Which action should the project manager take to ensure they are acting as a servant leader?
Options: Keep strict control of the team and their activities.
Serve as a backup in case a team member becomes ill.
Ensure the team follows all processes as defined without questioning them.
Understand the bottlenecks and support the team to remove them.
Questions.Explanation: Understand the bottlenecks and support the team to remove them A servant leader should be a coach and should help the team change a process if the team has found a better way to do it. They should understand bottlenecks and support the team to remove them. The other answer choices are incorrect because a servant leader should not control the team, nor serve as a backup, nor be a dictator.

Questions.Text: A project team is working on a complex technology project. A competitor introduces a product with a new feature, forcing the team to make changes to keep their product from becoming obsolete. The team uses software that is outdated and cannot accommodate the development of the new feature. As a result, the team must utilize new-to-market software. The team is eager to get started, but the project manager realizes that the team does not have any experience with the new software. What should the project manager do to address this knowledge gap?
Options: Train the team to use the new software.
Hire new team members to use the software.
Ignore the technological advancements.
Discuss the new features with the competitor.
Questions.Explanation: Train the team to use the new software. It is important to make sure project team members understand and fulfill their roles and responsibilities. This can include identifying gaps in knowledge and skills as well as strategies to address those gaps through training. Supporting people through change and helping address obstacles that can prevent success is also required. Some training is project specific, such as a faster way to accomplish specific work, other learning can be shared throughout the organization, such as training users on how to work with a new software application. In this case, the software is outdated, cannot accommodate the development of the new feature, and is being replaced, so training should be provided to ensure that the team has the knowledge necessary for this and future projects. Hiring new team members is incorrect for several reasons. Because the software is new-to-market, it may be difficult to hire people with extensive experience and the budget may not be able to support the hiring of new team members. Ignoring technological advancement is incorrect because it is not the appropriate way to address this situation. Discussing the new feature with the competitor is not appropriate and does not address the knowledge gap.

Questions.Text: A project manager notices that a critical team member has lost interest in the project. How should the project manager address this issue?
Options: Escalate this issue to the human resources (HR) manager.
Replace the team member with a more interested team member.
Schedule a meeting with the team member to identify the cause.
Increase the team member's salary.
Questions.Explanation: Schedule a meeting with the team member to identify the cause The first thing the project manager should do is determine the real cause of the team member's lack of interest. Then, the project manager will be able to motivate them appropriately. . The other answer choices are incorrect because they suggest the project manager take action without assessing the situation first.

Questions.Text: During the execution of repair work, the root cause of the damage was discovered to be failures that occurred during the construction phase. The same contractor who performed the construction work is performing the repairs. Because of this, the CEO wants to negotiate with the contractor for a reduced price. What should the project manager do?
Options: Negotiate with the contractor to cancel the repair work and pay a compensation for the previous work.
Negotiate the repair work with the contractor based on a shared-risk-reward relationship.
Proceed with the contractor without mentioning the issue since the previous work is already closed.
Proceed with the contractor and subtract the amount paid for supervision of the previous work.
Questions.Explanation: Negotiate the repair work with the contractor based on a shared-risk-reward relationship Since the current project does not fall in the same contract as the previous one, a collaborative approach is required. The project manger should negotiate the repair work with the contractor based on a shared-risk-reward relationship. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not benefit this situation and the repair work cannot be cancelled, as it does not fall in the original scope. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: O'Reilly Platform (No Date) //Project Procurement: A Real-World Guide for Procurement Skills, Ajay Bhargove , PMI/Chapter 6: The Game of Negotiation [Item As time lines for completing projects have shortened considerably compared to the last decade, when time to completion may have been as much as five years.

Questions.Text: A company is expanding its product manufacturing to a new country. The date the company will start operating is set and broadly communicated, but the project team is not sure if there is enough time to get all the product features implemented. What should the project manager do to ensure there is enough time to implement the critical features on the committed date?
Options: Add resources to the project team, as this will increase the velocity and enable the team to deliver more value.
Collaborate with the project team to estimate the size of the minimum viable product (MVP) functionalities.
Cut operations documentation because this can be completed after product delivery and operation starts.
Escalate the concern to the executive level and request a new and feasible project due date.
Questions.Explanation: Collaborate with the project team to estimate the size of the minimum viable product (MVP) functionalities This is a risk issue relating to scope and time. The project manager should work with the project team to evaluate the effort/size required to get the MVP features and functionality completed. This will provide the input for next steps. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not help the project manager ensure there is enough time to implement the critical features on the committed date.

Questions.Text: A customer is performing a weekly visit to a project site to inspect the final stage of product development. The quality manager alerts the project manager that the customer has been very unprofessional and reprimanded the team members during previous weekly visits. What should the project manager do to address this incident?
Options: Draft a letter complaining about the behavior of the customer inspector.
Allow the week to pass by, then send a complaint to the customer.
Discuss the matter with the project manager's senior management team.
Understand the situation by speaking with the team members and the customer.
Questions.Explanation: Understand the situation by speaking with the team members and the customer The project manager should attempt to resolve the conflict by defining the problem, collecting facts, analyzing the situation, and selecting the most appropriate course of action. The other answer choices are incorrect because they are reactions to an issue that may not be fully understood without the input of the team and the client.

Questions.Text: During a multidisciplinary project, a project manager learns that there are many conflicts about design among team members from different disciplines. What should the project manager do to ensure smooth delivery of the project?
Options: Identify the matter as a risk and check available budget reserves.
Ensure that the project sponsor is aware of the current situation.
Escalate to functional managers and seek resolution.
Facilitate team discussions to achieve the desired results.
Questions.Explanation: Facilitate team discussions to achieve the desired results The project manager must be proactive and facilitate resolution of any conflict within the multi-disciplinary project team. The other answer choices are incorrect. Identifying the matter as a risk or notifying the sponsor does not solve the problem; escalating the matter to functional managers is not correct, as it is the project manager's responsibility to facilitate problem solving among team members

Questions.Text: A stakeholder complains that one of the product's components is not easy to use. What should be the immediate next step for the project manager?
Options: Check if the component meets the design and performance specifications as per the acceptance criteria.
Clarify product acceptance criteria with the stakeholder.
Ask a user experience expert to explain to the stakeholder why the component was designed that way.
Raise a change request according to the stakeholder’ requirement.
Questions.Explanation: Check if the component meets the design and performance specifications as per the acceptance criteria The requirement and acceptance criteria should be agreed upon before the development work started. Any complaints about the requirement should be checked against the acceptance criteria first. If product design does not match with acceptance criteria, then the project manager should raise a change request after stakeholders review and discuss.

Questions.Text: During the planning phase of a project, the project manager is having trouble identifying stakeholders. Without input from the correct stakeholders, getting user feedback will be difficult. What should the project manager do?
Options: Review the stakeholder engagement plan.
Inform the project sponsors.
Refer to the Project Charter to identify stakeholders.
Review the project scope document.
Questions.Explanation: Refer to the Project Charter to identify stakeholders The project manager must identify and map the stakeholders while preparing for the project plan to gain input from the correct stakeholders.

Questions.Text: Critical project equipment is being produced by an overseas vendor. A quality survey revealed noncompliance with some of the project specifications. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Assign project personnel to oversee the vendor's facility to ensure compliance with the specifications.
Review the production contract to determine if the referenced specifications were included in the contract addendum.
Schedule a workshop with vendor representatives to provide an overview of the project specifications.
Schedule regular meetings with the vendor to personally control this situation.
Questions.Explanation: Review the production contract to determine if the referenced specifications were included in the contract addendum The project manager should review the production contract to determine if the referenced specifications were included in the contract addendum. Checking what was stipulated by the contract would be the best starting point because if some of the specifications were not included in the contract, the vendor could not be held accountable for compliance.

Questions.Text: During a project closing review meeting, the Project Manager notes that a software development task took longer to complete than was planned. Which action should the Project Manager take for continuous project improvement?
Options: Update the performance data in the lessons learned register.
Update the risk register so the problem will not happen in the future.
Update the problem in the issue log and distribute it to the project team.
Update the communications management plan with reviewed details of the problem.
Questions.Explanation: Update the performance data in the lessons learned register The project is at the project closing phase, so the issue is documented in the Lesson Learned Register for continuous project improvement. The other options are only employed in execution, not during closing.

Questions.Text: While closing a project, the client finds some defects in the deliverable. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Review the output against the acceptance criteria.
Extend the project until all deliverables are accepted.
Persuade the client to accept the output as is.
Pay the penalty for the defective item.
Questions.Explanation: Review the output against the acceptance criteria Reviewing the output against acceptance criteria is the appropriate next action as this will confirm why the defect wasn't raised earlier during the monitoring and controlling phase. Extending the project without approval is not the appropriate next action as it would delay the project timeline. Persuading the client to accept the output as is may compromise the quality of the project and is not appropriate. Paying the penalty for the defective item is not appropriate unless that clause is documented in contract with the client.

Questions.Text: An organizational transformation project has two workstreams running in parallel between corporate headquarters and an overseas office. Before kick-off, the project manager is scheduled to meet the overseas office leader. What should be the focus of the meeting?
Options: Inform the overseas office leader of the business' strategic value, the key deliverables, and their expected involvement.
Agree on the choice of procurement strategies to be used by the overseas office.
Help the overseas office leader communicate with corporate staff about the project.
Define and assign a subject matter expert (SME) for the project to represent the overseas office.
Questions.Explanation: Inform the overseas office leader of the business' strategic value, the key deliverables, and their expected involvement Since the meeting is scheduled before project kickoff, it is important to set expectations with all parties involved by informing the overseas office leader about the project business' strategic value, key deliverables, and the expected involvement. The other answer choices may happen after the project has been kicked off.

Questions.Text: During project execution, the project sponsor decides to replace a subject matter expert (SME) on the project. What should the project manager do?
Options: Negotiate with the project sponsor to retain the SME on the project.
Ensure there is sufficient knowledge transfer between the SME and their replacement.
Indicate to the new SME the project knowledge base as all information need it is there.
Meet with the project sponsor to explain the conflicts among the project team.
Questions.Explanation: Ensure there is sufficient knowledge transfer between the SME and their replacement The project manager should ensure sufficient knowledge transfer between the SME and their replacement. Knowledge transfer is required when there is a change of SMEs. The question explains that the SME is being replaced, so the best option in this situation would be to ensure sufficient knowledge transfer between the original SME and the replacement. The other answer choices are incorrect. Relying only on a knowledge base might not be sufficient to transfer all the SME's project knowledge; meeting with the project sponsor may not always be the right option as it depends on the sponsor's reason for replacement. Regardless of the reason for replacement, the project manager should ensure sufficient knowledge transfer between the two team members.

Questions.Text: After a few iterations, the project manager feels that even though the team is completing the work as planned, the team is not completely comfortable with some of the agile processes followed in the project. When should the project manager talk about this and make adjustments?
Options: At the next daily meeting.
At the lessons learned workshop at the end of the project.
At the next retrospective meeting.
At the planning meeting for the next iteration.
Questions.Explanation: At the next retrospective meeting Sharing observations and agreeing on adjustments for improvement to the way of working should be done at the retrospective meeting. Retrospective meetings provide an opportunity for the project team to review the way in which it works and to suggest changes to improve processes and efficiency. At a planning meeting for the next iteration is incorrect because that is not the nature of those meetings. Daily standup meetings are brief collaboration meetings during which the project team reviews its progress from the previous day, declares intentions for the current day, and highlights any obstacles encountered or anticipated. At a daily standup meeting is incorrect because that is not the nature of those meetings. An iteration planning meeting is used to clarify the details of the backlog items, acceptance criteria, and work effort required to meet an upcoming iteration commitment. At a lessons learned meeting at the end of the project is incorrect because that is not the nature of those meetings. A lessons learned meeting is used to identify and share the knowledge gained during a project with a focus on improving project team performance. This meeting can address situations that could have been handled better in addition to good practices and situations that produced very favorable outcomes. Raising it at the next retrospective meeting is preferred over the final lessons learned meeting since the adjusted behavior will increase value in the remaining iterations of the project.

Questions.Text: A project team has complete access to all project-related data, including visual data and information like the Gantt chart, prioritization matrix, story map, and other inputs. However, a project team member repeatedly requests more granular details on a work package than the Gantt chart indicates. The Gantt chart is confirmed to be comprehensive and current, and the other project team members feel that the current Gantt chart is sufficiently detailed. What should a project manager do to address this situation?
Options: Create a new communication channel with granular updates.
Ensure that the team member understands the granular information retrieval process.
Remind the project team member that the Gantt chart was the agreed format.
Continue to ignore the project team members' requests.
Questions.Explanation: Ensure that the team member understands the granular information retrieval process. Visual data and information are artifacts that organize and present data and information in a visual format, such as charts, graphs, matrices, and diagrams. Visualizing data makes it easier to absorb data and turn it into information. Visualization artifacts are often produced after data have been collected and analyzed. A Gantt chart is a bar chart that provides schedule information where activities are listed on the vertical axis, dates are shown on the horizontal axis, and activity durations are shown as horizontal bars placed according to start and finish dates. The project manager should ensure that the team member understands how to retrieve the granular update from the data as the member can self-serve. The team member should be empowered to get the level of information that they are authorized to get. The other answer choices are incorrect. Since it is only the specific project team member who wishes for more granular updates, creating a whole new communication channel for an individual may be a waste of resources. Ignoring the requests or reminding them that the team had agreed on the current reporting format of the Gantt chart would not address the situation. It may worsen the conflict with the team member who has an expectation gap not met.

Questions.Text: An organization is adopting an agile approach to fulfill their customer's requirements within a shorter time period and with better quality. Prior to that, the organization worked with specialized resources and project managers who were assigned to the project for a fixed amount of time. What should the organization change to better adopt the agile approach for resource planning?
Options: The product owner should request specialists to be assigned to the team only when they are required.
The agile team should consist of generalists and specialists who continuously learn and improve throughout the project duration.
The agile team should decide on which customer requirements will be developed and delivered.
The customer involvement will have to be reduced in order to avoid disruptions since the team will not always have the same set of members
Questions.Explanation: The agile team should consist of generalists and specialists who continuously learn and improve throughout the project duration The other answer choices are incorrect. Customer requirements and the level of customer involvement are not related to resource planning. Requesting for specialists only when required is not correct as the team collectively discusses and agrees on which skill sets are required for the product goals

Questions.Text: A program team, distributed across three different countries, is having a difficult time arranging meetings. The team members are also unclear about the program requirements and are having trouble getting their questions answered. What should be done to increase project success?
Options: Work independently until it is necessary to integrate the work.
Review the communications management plan and increase information sharing.
Increase frequency of team meetings to discuss any questions.
Bring questions to the functional managers to resolve issues across the team.
Questions.Explanation: Review the communications management plan and increase information sharing The project manager should review and improve the communications management plan to improve information sharing. The other answer choices are incorrect. These options do not increase project success since independent work is not efficient if members are unclear about the requirements; increasing frequency of meetings is also not likely to help if there is stated difficulty arranging them; and raising questions to functional management may transfer the work to functional managers unnecessarily. When teams are virtual/distributed, increased documentation helps ensure that requirements are understood by all involved. It can become virtually impossible to get everyone on all the calls, so relying solely on verbal communication limits success.

Questions.Text: A project manager is assigned to a new project. The requirements are still evolving, but there is a need to deliver business value quickly. What approach should the project manager take in this situation?
Options: Develop mitigation strategies to address the risks of evolving requirements.
Use a product backlog and complete the highest priority requirements first.
Ensure scope changes are documented in the requirements traceability matrix.
Develop a feature burndown chart and show progress on requirements
Questions.Explanation: Use a product backlog and complete the highest priority requirements first The project manager should select an iterative and incremental (Agile or hybrid) approach to manage evolving requirements. They can use a product backlog to complete the highest priority requirements and ensure that business value is delivered early and frequently.

Questions.Text: An experienced project manager is mentoring a new project manager assigned to their first project. As the new project manager becomes familiar with the project scope and the corresponding list of stakeholders, they are overwhelmed by the number of stakeholders. What should the experienced project manager tell the new project manager to do next?
Options: Perform a stakeholder analysis to determine relative importance.
Categorize key stakeholders using a workbook register.
Update the communications management plan with stakeholder preferences.
Prioritize stakeholders based on the stakeholder register
Questions.Explanation: Perform a stakeholder analysis to determine relative importance Stakeholder analysis results in a list of stakeholders and relevant information such as the level of attention required based on their positions in the organization, roles on the project, "stakes", expectations, attitudes, and their interests and influence in the project.

Questions.Text: An agile project is in the first of six iterations. The team is located globally, so the meetings are in a virtual environment. After the fifth daily meeting, the project manager realizes that not all members are actively participating. What should the project manager do?
Options: Ask the team members why participation is lacking.
Engage and guide the team to encourage all members to participate.
Ask each team member individually to participate in future meetings.
Allow the team to find their own way to fully participate.
Questions.Explanation: Engage and guide the team to encourage all members to participate The project manager should engage and guide the team to encourage all members to participate. The project manager has to be sure that the environment can accommodate member participation.

Questions.Text: A project manager is leading a complex software development project. The project team cannot agree on which process to use when solving a specific problem. What approach should the project manager take to help bring the team to consensus?
Options: Exert expert authority and define the path forward for the team.
Update the responsible, accountable, consult, and inform (RACI) matrix and assign the work to a different team.
Assign a new team lead who has experience in solving similar software development problems.
Facilitate a meeting and encourage team members to come up with potential solutions.
Questions.Explanation: Facilitate a meeting and encourage team members to come up with potential solutions The best option is to facilitate a meeting with the team to work out the solution. A collaborative approach not only inspires creative problem solving but also fosters team member engagement and participation. The other options are less than ideal as they do not promote team work.

Questions.Text: Some of the project's materials have been ordered from overseas. The project team has concerns that the materials may not reach the site on time due to a customs clearance delay that will affect the critical path. How should the project manager handle this concern?
Options: Ask the logistics team to arrange an expedited clearance.
Revisit the risk register and implement the respective risk response.
Identify the root cause of the delay and add it to the issue log.
Ensure the suppliers cover any additional costs due to the delay
Questions.Explanation: Revisit the risk register and implement the respective risk response Risks on critical deliveries should have been part of the risk register therefore PM should first revisit register since corrective action has been identified at the beginning of the project.

Questions.Text: A project manager was recently assigned to an investigation project. Due to the complexity of the project, the project manager is unsure if any regulation applies. How should the project manager determine which regulations apply to the project?
Options: Consult with a manager who has a higher level of authority for this decision.
Contact the project management office (PMO) manager as they are in charge of determining the regulations required for agile projects.
Schedule a meeting with key stakeholders, subject matter experts (SMEs), and the project team to better understand if any regulations are applicable.
Request a signed letter from the client to confirm if any regulations apply to any project deliverables.
Questions.Explanation: Schedule a meeting with key stakeholders, subject matter experts (SMEs), and the project team to better understand if any regulations are applicable The best option is to consult the experts available (SMEs), your resources, and any related stakeholders with expertise on the topic (Expert Judgement). The other answer choices are incorrect. PMO just provides guidance on the methodology to be used and not if any regulation needs to be applied. The high-level manager and the client may not be well informed of necessary regulations.

Questions.Text: A newly launched product prototype is receiving a number of customer complaints. To reduce negative feedback, the team comes up with an action plan. At the end of the quarter, however, the project manager finds there is no significant reduction in complaints. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Identify the key performers on the team and recognize them appropriately.
Identify the team members who lack the required skills and train them.
Revisit and reevaluate the project’s key performance indicators (KPIs).
Report the status to the executive management team and seek help.
Questions.Explanation: Revisit and reevaluate the project’s key performance indicators (KPIs) In an agile project, the project manager should revisit and reevaluate KPIs frequently to check the effectiveness of the action plan. The other answer choices are incorrect because they are not consistent with agile practices.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working on an internal project and notes that the visions/goals of other department leaders are not clear. This creates confusion and ambiguity within the project team, and it is becoming difficult to distinguish between the wish list and scope items. Which document should the project manager review to clarify this situation?
Options: Project Charter
Communications Management Plan
Risk Register
Stakeholder Engagement Plan
Questions.Explanation: Project Charter The project manager should review the project charter. The project is likely to fail if the documented vision and goal are not clear either by team members or stakeholders. The reason the project exists is because it was signed off by project sponsor and it has been in line with business strategies.

Questions.Text: During the execution of a project in a remote area, team members complain about the way one team member behaves after work. What should the project manager do to solve this problem?
Options: Call a meeting with the team to discuss the problem.
Plan a team-building session to discuss the problem.
Review the stakeholder's expectations and discuss this with the team.
Review the team norms and discuss this with the team.
Questions.Explanation: Review the team norms and discuss this with the team Since the behavior of the specific team member is after work hours, but it can affect the project if other team members see it as a problem, the team norms and ground rules must be discussed with the entire team. If the ground rules are not clear team members might have different expectations.

Questions.Text: A project manager is leading a new implementation project with a global project team. As team members are in different geographical locations and time zones, the project manager has to decide on the most effective team engagement strategy. What is the first action the project manager should take?
Options: Develop a communications management plan.
Set up daily conference calls to track work.
Ask all team members to utilize an online task board.
Ask team members to align their work schedules.
Questions.Explanation: Develop a communications management plan As the team members are in different geographical locations and time zones, it is very important that the project manager develops an effective engagement strategy first. As a result, review of team communication requirements is needed. This is done in the communication management plan. The other answer choices are incorrect. Setting up daily conference calls or asking the team members to align their work schedules may not be possible as the team is geographically distributed. Online task board may provide updates on the tasks, but primarily, there is always a need for engaging with stakeholders to keep the communication happening between team members.

Questions.Text: While working on a long project with many changes, a project manager is informed by a team member that some users are uncomfortable with certain product functionalities released in the previous iteration. Which action should the project manager take?
Options: Ensure that the team assignments are clear.
Obtain stakeholder feedback and team insights.
Communicate the changes to the team and external stakeholders.
Update the change log and issue log.
Questions.Explanation: Obtain stakeholder feedback and team insights Obtaining stakeholder feedback and team insights on each release is part of iterative approach to development, thus the project manager should do so. This enables alignment among stakeholder needs, expectations, and project objectives. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not result in a change to the project and clarifying team assignments does not address the issue at hand.

Questions.Text: A company has hired a project manager to implement an adaptive project framework. Project has made some progress, but the team is frustrated because client does not provide feedback or participate in defining acceptance criteria. What could have caused this situation?
Options: Development team has no experience in managing an adaptive project.
Client plans to implement project and does not need to participate.
Client does not understand the importance of their involvement.
Development team has not produced a complete prototype.
Questions.Explanation: Client does not understand the importance of their involvement In agile projects, the client often does not understand the importance of their involvement and teams engage with stakeholders directly rather than going through layers of management. Often client, user, and developer exchange information in a dynamic, co-creative process in order to facilitate timely, productive discussions and decision making that leads to more stakeholder involvement and higher satisfaction. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not address the issue of lack of client involvement. Even if the development team is new to adaptive projects, the client still needs to participate. The development team may not have produced complete prototype, however, the client still needs to define acceptance criteria.

Questions.Text: Part of the scope has already been delivered on a project. Unclear requirements on some of the remaining scope may have an impact on the scheduled completion. The sponsor still wants the project to meet the original deadline. How should the project manager approach the issue?
Options: Prioritize the most important features while clarifying the unclear requirements.
Initiate a change request for project sponsor approval for a scope change.
Log the issue and escalate it to the steering committee for a decision.
Acquire additional resources to complete all of the scope on time.
Questions.Explanation: Prioritize the most important features while clarifying the unclear requirements The project manager should prioritize the most important functionality within the original timelines in an iterative/agile cycle is the best approach. Unclear requirements calls for bringing team and product owner together to clarify expectations and value of requirement that may delay the project. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not suggest a way to meet the original deadline.

Questions.Text: During a daily standup, some team members realized they were not all prioritizing the requirements in the same way. This could impact the current iteration's goals. What should the project manager do to resolve this issue?
Options: Schedule an iteration review session and update backlog priorities.
Bring the team and product owner together to prioritize the value of requirements.
Ask the project sponsor to redefine the prioritized values.
Define priorities based upon the urgency of requirements and risks
Questions.Explanation: Bring the team and product owner together to prioritize the value of requirements The project manager should bring the team and product owner together to a backlog refinement meeting to prioritize the value of requirements. .Agile techniques are used to provide focus on prioritizing competing priorities in face-to-face interaction with product owner. In order to better understand the value of requirements added to the product in each iteration, the product owner and team should think about how to optimize the flow of value.

Questions.Text: A client's project sponsor assigns a contact person to the project manager. Additional intermediate reports and details are being asked by the contact person increasing the overhead of preparing the progress reports. Yet the client’s project sponsor complains of non-receipt of progress reports during a monthly status meeting What should the project manager do to address this situation?
Options: Compile all previous intermediate reports, summarize them, and send them directly to the client’s project sponsor.
Set up a review meeting with the client's project sponsor and contact person to address the communication gaps.
Explain the content and frequency of progress reporting to the contact and client's project sponsor.
Provide the client's project sponsor access to the company’s project management dashboard.
Questions.Explanation: Set up a review meeting with the client's project sponsor and contact person to address the communication gaps Clearly, the information needs of the project sponsor is not being addressed by the contact person nor by the project manager. The project manager should analyze the need of the project sponsor but this cannot be done without the support of the contact person. Hence, the project manager should discuss with both the project sponsor and the contact person on what the progress report should contain and how often to report the status. PMBoK Sixth Edition in chapter 4 states: "4.5.3.1 WORK PERFORMANCE REPORTS Work performance information is combined, recorded, and distributed in a physical or electronic form in order to create awareness and generate decisions or actions. Work performance reports are the physical or electronic representation of work performance information intended to generate decisions, actions, or awareness. They are circulated to the project stakeholders through the communication processes as defined in the project communications management plan. Examples of work performance reports include status reports and progress reports. Work performance reports can contain earned value graphs and information, trend lines and forecasts, reserve burndown charts, defect histograms, contract performance information, and risk summaries. They can be presented as dashboards, heat reports, stop light charts, or other representations useful for creating awareness and generating decisions and actions" The correct response is to set up a joint meeting with the Client's Project Sponsor and his nominated 'Contact-Person' to resolve this communications issue. The Distractors are clearly and correctly worded and are hence accepted

Questions.Text: A project manager has a new project. The work breakdown structure (WBS) and sequence of activities have been defined, but the team has difficulty estimating the duration of each activity. Which approach should the project manager take to ensure the estimates are reliable?
Options: Ask each team member to estimate the duration of each activity.
Consider the project schedule constraints and estimate the activities accordingly.
Access performance records of similar, past projects and use them as a reference.
Consult other project managers for the duration estimate of each activity.
Questions.Explanation: Access performance records of similar, past projects and use them as a reference Making all your members estimate each activity will not be effective unless all your members are expert. It will not be a reliable approach to do bottom-up estimation as well. The best approach is to check with the PMO records of past similar projects and use them as reference. PMBoK Sixth Edition in Chapter 5 and 6 states: "5.4.3.1 SCOPE BASELINE The scope baseline is the approved version of a scope statement, WBS, and its associated WBS dictionary, which can be changed only through formal change control procedures and is used as a basis for comparison. It is a component of the project management plan WBS. The WBS is a hierarchical decomposition of the total scope of work to be carried out by the project team to accomplish the project objectives and create the required deliverables. Each descending level of the WBS represents an increasingly detailed definition of the project work. WBS dictionary. The WBS dictionary is a document that provides detailed deliverable, activity, and scheduling information about each component in the WBS. The WBS dictionary is a document that supports the WBS. Most of the information included in the WBS dictionary is created by other processes and added to this document at a later stage 6.4 ESTIMATE ACTIVITY DURATIONS Estimate Activity Durations is the process of estimating the number of work periods needed to complete individual activities with estimated resources. The key benefit of this process is that it provides the amount of time each activity will take to complete. This process is performed throughout the project The Estimate Activity Durations process requires an estimation of the amount of work effort required to complete the activity and the amount of available resources estimated to complete the activity 6.4.2.4 THREE-POINT ESTIMATING The accuracy of single-point duration estimates may be improved by considering estimation uncertainty and risk" For the given answer options the correct response, therefore, is: "Access performance records of similar, past projects and use them as a reference" The Distractors correctly worded and are accepted

Questions.Text: A large transformation project has moved its project management office (PMO) to a virtual environment. There have been performance issues with the PMO team regarding the daily tasks they perform, which are now affecting the project. Which three actions should the project manager take to improve the performance of the PMO team? (Choose three)
Options: Nurture confidence among the PMO team members by providing them with the required training on project management tools.
Hire a new senior manager for the virtual teams to improve collaboration between departments.
Suggest that management dissolves the virtual team or moves the PMO to a colocated area.
Build a collaborative environment by having regular connections among the PMO team members.
Provide access to communication tools to help the virtual teams connect better with the stakeholders.
Questions.Explanation: Nurture confidence among the PMO team members by providing them with the required training on project management tools, Build a collaborative environment by having regular connections among the PMO team members and Provide access to communication tools to help the virtual teams connect better with the stakeholders The project manager is interested in improving the performance of the virtual PMO team. Providing the team with training that nurtures their confidence, building a collaborative environment through regular connections among the team members and providing access to communication tools towards better team connection will help towards achieving this. The other answer choices are incorrect. Dissolving the virtual team however does not help improve the PMO in its current environment and hiring a senior manager to improve collaboration between departments does not address issues of performance within the PMO virtual team.

Questions.Text: On an agile project, a team member has been taking smaller, easier user stories from the backlog, and not necessarily those with the highest priority. How should this be addressed?
Options: Remind the whole team that the highest priority items need to be taken from the backlog first.
Direct the team member to take specific, more challenging user stories in the next iteration.
Meet with the team member to understand the reason and tailor the workload approach, if required.
Retrain the team member on the core technologies used to build the product to address the skill gap.
Questions.Explanation: Meet with the team member to understand the reason and tailor the workload approach, if required The project manager needs to understand the root cause before taking action. The other answer choices are incorrect because they suggest that the project manager should direct and retrain the team member without first assessing the issue.

Questions.Text: During the design phase, a project manager realizes that the project will benefit from using adaptive tools. The effectiveness of this approach has been proven in past projects inside the organization. What should the project manager do first?
Options: Confirm team capabilities before introducing adaptive tools and artifacts to the project.
Contact the project sponsor and request new team members who are familiar with adaptive projects.
Freeze the design stage and look for an external resource to run iterative design at extra cost.
Update project documents to include adaptive tools and artifacts and plan the first iterative session.
Questions.Explanation: Confirm team capabilities before introducing adaptive tools and artifacts to the project The project manager should first confirm team capabilities before introducing adaptive tools and artifacts to the project. The other answer choices are incorrect because they are not the first steps. Without knowing if the team has capabilities to deal with adaptive tools, the project manager may compromise some aspects of the project without the need to do so. Also, it is part of the project manager responsibilities to guarantee that the team has the necessary training to perform their roles in the project.

Questions.Text: A project manager has been assigned to a project that is currently late and over budget. The project uses a predictive approach integrated with agile iterations for the most critical part of the scope. During the project assessment, the project manager notices that the team is afraid of making mistakes and is seeking individual recognition, while lacking accountability. How should the project manager improve productivity?
Options: Develop a performance management plan for each project team member and share the performance results with the team.
Enable collaboration, create common goals, and celebrate group achievements, while providing group rewards.
Reinforce and communicate the next milestone, following individual performance and execution on a detailed level.
Request approval to replace a few team members so the project team can understand the importance of meeting goals.
Questions.Explanation: Enable collaboration, create common goals, and celebrate group achievements, while providing group rewards The project manager needs to create a collaborative environment that focuses on common goals and group rewards, and moving away from working in silos. The other answer choices are incorrect. Developing a performance management plan for each project team member and sharing the performance results with the team or creating milestones at an individual level will only deteriorate the working relationship among the team members. Replacing a few team members may not improve the team morale or productivity unless the team is focused on common goals and working together as a team and focusing on group achievements.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working on a global project with team members in several different countries. To manage communications, the project manager plans to hold virtual meetings and on-site visits once a month. What should the project manager do next while working with this team?
Options: Ask the IT manager of each site to manage the technical components of the virtual meetings.
Request the team members' résumés and become familiar with the teams' experiences.
Propose and establish a team charter with the rules and principles to be followed.
Request the budget for weekly in-person meetings with the entire team to allow for direct contact.
Questions.Explanation: Propose and establish a team charter with the rules and principles to be followed The next thing the project manager should do is establish a team charter. The team charter is a document that establishes the team values, agreements, and operating guidelines. The project manager can use this document to solve or avoid problems in the team. Facilitating project team communication, problem solving, and the process of coming to a consensus may include working with the project team to develop a project team charter and a set of operating guidelines or project team norms. The other answer choices are incorrect. The team has already been established so their resumes would have already been reviewed; weekly colocation will be more costly and time-consuming than monthly meetups; asking the IT manager to manage technical components will not directly benefit teamwork. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: Managing Virtual Teams: Getting the Most from Wikis, Blogs, and Other Collaborative Tools (No Date) /M. Katherine Brown, Brenda Huettner and Charlene James-Tanny/Chapter 2 Setting Up a Virtual Team/Chapter 2 Setting Up a Virtual Team [Item Structure, Roles, and Responsibilities The structure of the team and its relationship to the corporate hierarchy forms the framework upon which the roles and responsibilities hang. The structure of the team defines how each functional area will work together, as well as who shows up for reviews, who manages changes, who works with whom, and how often meetings are held. (See Chapter 7 for more about the review process.)]

Questions.Text: A project manager has developed a schedule for a project involving various departments. The project manager met with each team member to develop a work breakdown structure (WBS). However, after the WBS was finalized, the project manager discovers that some tasks are redundant. What should the project manager have done to avoid this issue?
Options: Enlisted the help of subject matter experts (SMEs) to provide scheduling guidance.
Organized group sessions for all team members to review the WBS together.
Improved the communication channels with all team members.
Prepared a task assignment matrix to represent the WBS.
Questions.Explanation: Organized group sessions for all team members to review the WBS together All the project planning activities should be discussed during the joint planning sessions. During these sessions team members will share their approaches and this will minimize activities' overlap, increase awareness among the team members.

Questions.Text: While gathering requirements for the project scope, the project manager realizes that some of the stakeholder requirements have a conflict. What should the project manager do to define a clear scope?
Options: Assist affected stakeholders in reaching consensus.
Follow the recommendation of the technical consultant.
Escalate to the project sponsor to make a decision.
Conduct a meeting with the disagreeing parties only.
Questions.Explanation: Assist affected stakeholders in reaching consensus The project manager has to be proactive to reach consensus between project team and stakeholders. All parties should be involved so that consensus can be reached. The project manager must use all necessary communication techniques to gather and ensure requirements are clear. The other options are incorrect. They do not involve all parties to resolve the conflict.

Questions.Text: A member of a development project team informs the project manager that the operating system is not going to be installed on time due to a late upgrade by the supplier. What should the project manager do first?
Options: Determine the impact of the situation.
Modify the schedule to reflect the situation.
Inform the stakeholders about the problem.
Have the project team brainstorm a solution.
Questions.Explanation: Determine the impact of the situation The project manager must quantify an impact in order to understand the adjustments needed in the project in terms of schedule, budget, and/or resources. Only after the impact is assessed can the project manager implement the appropriate responses to maintain optimal performance.

Questions.Text: A company has decided to utilize an international virtual team due to a lack of locally skilled resources. What can the project manager do to build an effective project communications management plan?
Options: Study the impact of cultural differences on communication.
Adopt an Email "push" communication approach with the team.
Set up a project folder in the cloud and endorse a pull communication approach for the team.
Assign a communications manager to the project.
Questions.Explanation: Study the impact of cultural differences on communication Cultural awareness is a first step in understanding the differences between individuals, groups, and organizations and adapting the project’s communication strategy in the context of these differences. You need to understand what makes cultures and communication styles different from one another to avoid miscommunications and enhance effective communications. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not address the potential root cause of communication issues.

Questions.Text: During project implementation, the project purpose is being questioned by a stakeholder who wants to pause the project to address a new process that may affect the final deliverable. What is one of the first things the project manager should do?
Options: Use a power/interest grid to determine the stakeholder's ability to influence the planning or execution of a project or the project's outcomes.
Confer with the project sponsor to determine next steps.
Defer any changes to a future project in order to meet the deliverables in the current project.
Revisit the acceptance criteria with the stakeholder to ensure the quality level is appropriate.
Questions.Explanation: Use a power/interest grid to determine the stakeholder's ability to influence the planning or execution of a project or the project's outcomes An impact/influence grid supports a grouping of stakeholders according to their level of authority (power), level of concern about the project's outcomes (interest), ability to influence the outcomes of the project (influence), or ability to cause changes to the project's planning or execution.

Questions.Text: The project manager of an agile project noticed that many stakeholders are not familiar with the iterative approach, and are either tentatively attending reviews or skipping them altogether. Which action should the project manager take?
Options: Review and update the communications management plan.
Identify gaps and mentor stakeholders on the iterative approach.
Record the iteration review and share it with stakeholders.
Build a RACI matrix and develop a training plan for stakeholders.
Questions.Explanation: Identify gaps and mentor stakeholders on the iterative approach Stakeholders should be aware of what iterative approach means, in order to attend iteration reviews, and should be mentored if they do not understand what it means. Thus, the project manager should identify gaps and mentor stakeholders on the iterative approach. The other answer choices are incorrect as they do not address the stakeholder's gaps in knowledge

Questions.Text: Which of the following project artifacts do not utilize the WBS as an input?
Options: Project schedule
Project scope
Risk management plan
Quality plan
Questions.Explanation: Project scope WBS is developed based on the agreed scope.

Questions.Text: Project manager B has been assigned to take over a project from Project Manager A. Project Manager A informs Project Manager B that the project is on schedule, only because Project Manager A constantly pushes the team to perform. What is the first action Project Manager B should take as the new project manager?
Options: Check the risk status.
Check the cost performance.
Determine a management strategy.
Tell the team the new objectives.
Questions.Explanation: Determine a management strategy

Questions.Text: In a program, several groups are identifying problem solving techniques and each group presents a proposal. Among those listed, which is valid?
Options: Performing project health checks
Reading literature related to your industry
Establishing a project suggestion box
All of the above
Questions.Explanation: All of the above Project team members and other stakeholders can provide information, suggestions, and assistance in working with all the various forms of uncertainty. To optimize value delivery, project teams should use problem solving as well as a holistic-thinking approach to changes and unplanned events. Projects can be challenging from the perspectives of technical challenges, environmental influences, and interpersonal interactions. Recommending a new approach to a problem or a way of working can be intimidating. Likewise, it can be challenging to disagree with a subject matter expert or someone with greater authority. However, demonstrating the courage that it takes to make a suggestion, disagree, or try something new enables a culture of experimentation and communicates to others that it is safe to be courageous and try new approaches.

Questions.Text: Which is an essential component for maintaining the support and commitment of all stakeholders?
Options: Honesty
Effective communication
Transparency
Reliability
Questions.Explanation: Effective communication

Questions.Text: A project manager's team has identified the lack of proper knowledge transfer expectations in the project. Which of the obstacles does not apply in this situation?
Options: A systematic review of the completed project
Personally handled failures and mistakes
Insufficient individual motivation to document the lessons learned
Sparse leadership involvement to promote knowledge transfer
Questions.Explanation: A systematic review of the completed project

Questions.Text: In an agile environment, a project leader should always consider a time for open and free discussions between team members on how the team can increase their performance. Why is this practice important for the team?
Options: Teams should take actions to continuously increase productivity
Teams should continuously experiment new ways of doing things
Teams should continuously seek to improve their processes
Teams should continuously push their members to work harder
Questions.Explanation: Teams should continuously seek to improve their processes Empowering the team to take decisions on how their processes can be improved will lead to better productivity. The Project Lead must coach the team on how they can analyze and improve their processes as a team and take accountability and pride in improving the processes. Experimentation and taking actions alone may increase risks. Pushing team members to continuously work harder may lower moral and motivation.

Questions.Text: A project manager is managing an agile project. The PMO manager is not familiar with agile delivery. Which of the following statements describe the agile approach?
Options: Responding to change over following a plan
Customer collaboration over following a plan
Responding to change over processes and tools
Customer collaboration over comprehensive testing
Questions.Explanation: Responding to change over following a plan The main characteristic of Agile delivery is that embrace change over strictly following a detailed plan

Questions.Text: After an annual staff review, discussing project management and soft skills knowledge, a company made a decision to train all staff members to face new challenges. The method to train all staff members will be on a website where all content, exams and interaction methods are available. What method is being used for training purpose?
Options: Pull
Push
Interactive
Interpersonal
Questions.Explanation: Pull In Pull communication, the sender creates information or content that receivers can passively access[pull content] at their convenience. It is not real time Here, the method used to train all staff members is posting all the content, exams and interactive methods on the website and the staff members will log into the website to access the content. Hence this option is the correct answer. Push communication, interactive communication or interpersonal is more real time communication methods. Hence all these three options are incorrect.

Questions.Text: After consultation with executive stakeholders, it was decided that the project manager would replace a vendor while the project is in the execution phase. The new vendor simply has more domain knowledge and was not identified earlier, but is probably the right vendor candidate now. Which of the following documents would contain the best information for the new vendor?
Options: Request for Information (RFI)
Request for Quote (RFQ)
Request for Proposal (RFP)
Request for Contract (RFC)
Questions.Explanation: Request for Proposal (RFP) The RFP contains all the functional and non functional requirements and the acceptance criteria. The other documents are brief from the scope point of view RFC and RFQ may have the RFP as a reference and RFI can focus on a specific aspect only. An RFP is used when there is a problem in the project and the solution is not easy to determine. This is the most formal of the “request for” documents and has strict procurement rules for content, timeline, and seller responses.

Questions.Text: You are the project manager in an organization that is implementing agile delivery. You have experience as a scrum master and will perform this role in addition to the project management responsibilities. One of the team members wants to know what the main focus of a servant leader is. What will be your response?
Options: Client satisfaction and value delivered demonstration.
Growth and well-being of people and communities they belong to.
Stakeholders expectation management and communication.
Productivity and efficiency to achieve commitments made.
Questions.Explanation: Growth and well-being of people and communities they belong to. A servant leader seeks leadership opportunities to serve others. They measure success through the growth, development, and well-being of team members and the communities they belong to. The other answer choices options are more applicable to a traditional leader, but not the main focus of servant leadership.

Questions.Text: What are the four team-building leadership styles?
Options: Visionary, affiliative, coaching and participative
Visionary, affiliative, coaching and pace setting
Visionary, affiliative, coaching and directive
Visionary, affiliative, coaching and high waters
Questions.Explanation: Visionary, affiliative, coaching and participative -team builder leadership styles, focusing more on achievements than relationship Visionary, affiliative, coaching and participative are all related to team building leadership styles. Pace setting, directive and high waters are non.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working on a long-term project in a complex environment. The project manager's focus is on maintaining healthy team work and collaboration. What conflict resolution strategy should be used within the project team?
Options: Problem solving
Compromising
Accommodating
Consultation
Questions.Explanation: Problem solving It is recommended to avoid the conflict or problem by solving it before it becomes an issue that could impact the team work and environment.

Questions.Text: An internal project has delivered its scope on time, within budget, and with the expected quality. The client was satisfied with the result and it enabled them to launch the new product to market. However, the product did not attract the expected interest and did not cover the investment made. The project team is aware of these results and it is impacting their morale to deliver the next project. How should the project manager handle this situation? Choose 2 answers.
Options: Have a discussion with the team to explain that the product success is not a direct result of the project and it does not reflect their performance
Have a meeting with the business to make sure they understand the project met its objectives
No action is necessary, as the project manager is only responsible for the project deliverables.
Have a conversation with the business sponsor to validate the strategy for the new project based on this result.
Questions.Explanation: Have a discussion with the team to explain that the product success is not a direct result of the project and it does not reflect their performance have a conversation with the business sponsor to validate the strategy for the new project based on this result

Questions.Text: A digital transformation project has reached its end. A customer complained about missing requirements. What approach should the project manager take to address the customers concern?
Options: Take a sign off on current requirement and treat the scope gap as a new requirement
Accept the new changes and request an extension
Do not accept the new changes and close the current project.
Use the Ideation approach to address the problem.
Questions.Explanation: Use the Ideation approach to address the problem https://www.projectmanagement.com/articles/675586/An-Introduction-to-Ideation-2-0-in-Practice This approach eases the burden on the IT department by empowering the business to mature ideas and kick off the process of creating eventual solutions. Done correctly, this will eliminate effort and the often-lengthy time frames associated with pitching an underdeveloped idea to IT. In addition, it will have the added benefit of reducing the burden of upfront paperwork and evidence. Ordinarily, the IT team would want a level of detail for the solution that may yet to have been broadly considered or articulated. This slows down the process further, often resulting in scope creep and an underdeveloped solution being built. Given the inherent suitability of citizen development platforms for this purpose, Ideation 2.0 presents an approach that combines brainstorming and real-time developing, which enables organizations to drive significant value in condensed time frames. Looked at another way, Ideation 2.0 is about avoiding the pitfall of taking a snapshot of an idea in its infancy and handing that over to the IT department to build. It is about recognizing that sustaining ideation during the build phase is something that should be encouraged. Citizen development can harness this approach to shape, and ultimately deliver a better end product in less time.

Questions.Text: A project manager is on a project that is part of a business transformation initiative impacting most of the 10,000 employees in the company. The scope of the project is the improvement of collaboration between teams by implementing a new cloud platform that will replace an 20-year old communication tool. What will be the most critical success factor?
Options: Good quality. Go Live should have zero critical defects.
Good user interface developed by talented graphical artists
Good change management planning and execution
Good change control process with fast approvals
Questions.Explanation: Good change management planning and execution Any project that impacts a large number of people need significant change management to ensure that the change won't be rejected. Regardless of how good the quality is and how beautiful the User Interface is the success rate is the adoption of the new process and the tools supporting it.

Questions.Text: A project manager conducted an analysis with relevant stakeholders to validate project alignment with organizational strategy and expected business value. Some of the key stakeholders missed the analysis session, and hence, their input was not factored in. What is the main impact of these absences on the project?
Options: Important project approval was not received.
There is inconsistent input on benefits from various key stakeholders.
There is inconsistent input on risks from various key stakeholders.
Conflict in subsequent phases of the project may result.
Questions.Explanation: Conflict in subsequent phases of the project may result Conflicts can be generated because the stakeholders have different expectation and sometimes conflicting interests. This could lead to a dispute in the project. The other answer choices are incorrect because they don't apply to this situation. As per the scenario, this is an analysis meeting, so no approval is missed due to absence of key stakeholders. There is no inconsistency of inputs, in fact, we are missing inputs on benefits and risks of key stakeholders.

Questions.Text: A project manager is working on a deployment project to replace some equipment that will soon become obsolete. A review of the schedule and cost has revealed that a 5-month delay is likely. How should the project manager address risks from the delay?
Options: Analyze the business impact in order to reprioritize the deliverables.
Request to negotiate with the vendors for a longer support period.
Ask to order new equipment immediately so there will be no support issue.
Acknowledge these constraints and take them into account when planning.
Questions.Explanation: Analyze the business impact in order to reprioritize the deliverables The correct answer is to analyze the business impact in order to reprioritize the deliverables. In order to address the risks related to the delay, they must first be analyzed. An analysis must be done to determine the impact of the risks in order to plan appropriate responses. The project manager must evaluate the importance of each risk in order to categorize and prioritize individual risks for further attention. The other answer choices are incorrect because they are reactionary and do not fully address risks from the delay. They also do not address the impact that the risks may have on the project, the organization, or previously identified risks.

Questions.Text: A project manager is overseeing a procurement project, and the company has a dedicated procurement management team that handles contracts. The quarterly report identifies six contracts that do not align with the company objectives. What will be the most suitable action for the project manager to take?
Options: Keep the six contracts even though they do not align with the company objectives.
Recommend to terminate all six contracts that are not aligning with the company objectives.
Ask the project sponsor to revise the requirements of the contracts.
Request the customer to revise the requirements of the contracts.
Questions.Explanation: Recommend to terminate all six contracts that are not aligning with the company objectives Contracts should be terminated if they do not align with company objectives.

Questions.Text: A virtual team is midway through a sprint of a complex task and is ahead of schedule. During the daily standup, which often tends to end early, a new team member interrupts and asks everyone to define their role and what they worked on prior to this sprint. How should project manager handle this situation?
Options: Address the situation immediately and make sure the new team member has all the information they need to be successful.
Respectfully inform the team member that this would be best taken care of in another meeting.
Team members are not supposed to interrupt in meetings and project manager can ask them to keep quiet.
Allow the new team member to ask the team whatever questions are needed for clarification.
Questions.Explanation: Respectfully inform the team member that this would be best taken care of in another meeting A standup is not the place or time to have a status meeting. The project manager should respectfully inform the team member that this would be best taken care of in another meeting. The other answer choices are incorrect because they either accommodate the interruption or rudely shut it down

Questions.Text: During a progress meeting, the project team learns that an identified risk materialized. The project manager decides to execute the risk response plan, but the contingency reserve does not cover the total budget for that plan. What should the project manager do?
Options: Ask the project sponsor to apply the management reserve to cover the rest of the amount.
Ask the project sponsor to provide an additional budget to account for the additional risk response.
Issue a change request to modify the scope, cost, and schedule baselines to support the new plan.
Use the contingency reserve and study how to reduce the project budget to cover the remaining work.
Questions.Explanation: Issue a change request to modify the scope, cost, and schedule baselines to support the new plan The implementation of a risk response plan, supposes a change on the current project management plan. A change request will result in asking for the budget needed, re-schedule, and new activities on the scope. Hence this option is the correct answer. Management reserve is only applicable for unidentified risks in the project. Hence the option of asking the project sponsor to apply the management reserve to the rest of the project does not apply and hence this option is incorrect. Since the contingency reserve does not cover the total budget, the case of asking project sponsor for additional budget or using the contingency reserve is not applicable. Hence these options are incorrect.

Questions.Text: A non-colocated project team just finished a sprint with complex tasks ahead of schedule.
