During the retrospective, which often end early for this team, a new member of the team interrupts and asks everyone to explain their roles and what they are planning on working on for the next sprint. What should the project manager do?
Options: Address the situation immediately and answer the new team member directly.
Inform the team member that this would be best handled in another meeting.
Ask each team member to explain their role and what they will work on for the next sprint.
Set up one-on-one meetings with each team member and the new team member.
Questions.Explanation: Inform the team member that this would be best handled in another meeting The best way for the project manager to handle the interruption is to inform the team member that this would be best handled in another meeting, like a backlog refinement for sprint planning, not the retrospective. The other answer choices are incorrect as they suggest that the project manager should accommodate the new team members question at an inappropriate time.

Questions.Text: A project manager has requested to purchase the same parts from two vendors. Vendor A informs the project manager that they fulfilled a similar order two months ago, which was 2% under the original planned cost. Vendor B has delivered consistently on the orders but has announced a pricing increase. How should the project manager evaluate this situation?
Options: Review the agreements and procurement documentation for vendor B to force them to keep the original pricing.
Evaluate the contract clauses to reduce the orders from vendor B, and buy them from vendor A to improve project indicators.
Use the metrics from vendor A to request better pricing from vendor B, keeping both providers as a risk mitigation strategy.
Review the work performance information of both vendors against their contracts to find the preferred supplier.
Questions.Explanation: Review the work performance information of both vendors against their contracts to find the preferred supplier To evaluate the situation, the project manager should review the work performance information of both vendors against their contracts to find the preferred supplier. If the project manager decides to go for an alternative source, they must first review the vendors' past performance. The other answer choices are incorrect because they are not the best way to evaluate and select the preferred vendor.

Questions.Text: A client has contracted a company to manage a large project in an agile environment with many subcontractors. The client is giving direct orders to the subcontractors, rather than to the contractor's project manager. This has created conflict within the team. What should the project manager do to prevent this situation from occurring again?
Options: Keep the team informed about client expectations.
Invite the client to the team planning meeting.
Tell the team to avoid conflicts with the client.
Ask the client not to give direct orders to the subcontractors.
Questions.Explanation: Invite the client to the team planning meeting The client's actions can be prevented by ensuring that the client understands the team's agreed approach to allocating work within the team. This understanding can be achieved when the client attends a team meeting to see how work is being allocated and reviewed. The other answer choices are incorrect. The option to ask the client not to give direct orders to the subcontractors may increase conflict within the parties if the client does not have a clear understanding of the expected behavior. The options to keep the team informed of client expectations, and telling the team to avoid conflicts with the client are not related to the conflict, hence they are not correct options.

Questions.Text: After a few iterations, the project manager feels that even though the team is completing the work as planned, the team is not completely comfortable with some of the agile processes followed in the project. When should the project manager talk about this and make adjustments?
Options: At the next daily meeting.
At the lessons learned workshop at the end of the project.
At the next retrospective meeting.
At the planning meeting for the next iteration.
Questions.Explanation: At the next retrospective meeting Sharing observations and agreeing on adjustments for improvement to the way of working should be done at the retrospective meeting. Retrospective meetings provide an opportunity for the project team to review the way in which it works and to suggest changes to improve processes and efficiency. At a planning meeting for the next iteration is incorrect because that is not the nature of those meetings. Daily standup meetings are brief collaboration meetings during which the project team reviews its progress from the previous day, declares intentions for the current day, and highlights any obstacles encountered or anticipated. At a daily standup meeting is incorrect because that is not the nature of those meetings. An iteration planning meeting is used to clarify the details of the backlog items, acceptance criteria, and work effort required to meet an upcoming iteration commitment. At a lessons learned meeting at the end of the project is incorrect because that is not the nature of those meetings. A lessons learned meeting is used to identify and share the knowledge gained during a project with a focus on improving project team performance. This meeting can address situations that could have been handled better in addition to good practices and situations that produced very favorable outcomes. Raising it at the next retrospective meeting is preferred over the final lessons learned meeting since the adjusted behavior will increase value in the remaining iterations of the project.

Questions.Text: A project manager is assigned to take over an ongoing project. During the quality review, it is found that 40% of the deliverables have defects and require rework, with an expected worsening trend. What should be the project manager's first step?
Options: Evaluate the cost of the rework.
Review the quality management plan.
Review the user stories.
Perform a root cause analysis.
Questions.Explanation: Perform a root cause analysis Evaluating the cost of re-work is required by the project manager, but the first step should be to perform a root cause analysis since the defects identified are expected to increase over time. This would allow the project manager to identify the root cause of these defects and agree on the next course of action. That would then feed into evaluating the cost of rework, which should include addressing the root cause. The options on reviewing the user stories and quality management plan do not address the issue of defective deliverables, hence they are not correct.

Questions.Text: In the most recent sprint review meeting, the client announced that they need to implement two of the five modules committed for the project in a few weeks. What should the team do?
Options: Conduct brainstorming.
Use the nominal group technique.
Conduct a release plan meeting.
Use prototypes.
Questions.Explanation: Conduct a release plan meeting The correct option is to conduct a release plan meeting where the team discusses all the modules and prioritizes them to deliver the most value possible. This would allow them to re-prioritize the work to deliver the two modules first. The other options are valid responses but would not directly help the team prioritize the work to complete the first two modules ahead of schedule.

Questions.Text: In the middle of a release, the project manager is replaced. Feedback provided by the team to the new project manager hinted at team friction caused by the previous project manager's constant demands for rapid delivery. What approach should the new project manager adopt to quickly establish credibility with the team?
Options: Do what the team needs to be done, maintain the action log, and provide feedback.
Help the team succeed with excellent service, on-time commitments, and an adequate budget.
Assess the culture of the team, and observe and interact with them.
Have the team explain what is needed, create tasks, and report progress on those tasks.
Questions.Explanation: Assess the culture of the team, and observe and interact with them To quickly establish credibility, it is important that the new project manager first observe and understand how the team works and how to best serve them. The other answer choices are incorrect. Helping the team succeed with excellent service, on-time commitments, and making sure that there is an adequate budget to cover the project scope is part of the project manager's role and is implicit and these actions will not help the project manager to quickly establish credibility with the team which is the primary objective. Going by the team's needs and wants and creating tasks and reports will not serve the primary objective. The team members may have conflicting views, wants, and needs. Ultimately, it is important to observe the team to identify where impactful changes can be made and to ensure that any changes are aligned with the culture of the organization and best support the intended outcome.

Questions.Text: An organization makes an acquisition of a similar product halfway through the development cycle of a potential product. This might create an overlap in the product portfolio. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Assess the feasibility and viability of the project with the product owner.
Continue to develop the product for the benefit of the organization.
Discuss the potential impact of the acquisition with team members.
Halt the project until a clear path emerges for the product roadmap.
Questions.Explanation: Assess the feasibility and viability of the project with the product owner The project manager should assess the impact of the new acquisition and determine next steps with the product owner. This would ensure that expectations are aligned. The other answer choices are incorrect. Continuing with the product development or halting the work is not reasonable, since the action may result in wastage of effort or time if the organization's intentions are not aligned. Discussing the potential impact is also valid. However, the discussion should be with the product owner first. The team members may join the discussion later for the impact analysis.

Questions.Text: After emailing a recent project status report, the project manager learns that a number of recipients are no longer involved with the project. The email distribution list was based on the communications management plan. Which action should the project manager take first to verify the distribution list for future status reports?
Options: Review and update the stakeholder engagement plan.
Review and update the communications management plan.
Review and update the project management plan.
Review and update the resource management plan.
Questions.Explanation: Review and update the stakeholder engagement plan The stakeholder management plan should be reviewed and updated when current stakeholders are no longer with the project. The other answer choices are incorrect. Update the communications management plan should happen after the stakeholder engagement plan is reviewed and updated; updating the project management plan and resource management plan may not be required if there are no expected changes or impact to those artifacts. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: Information Technology Project Management (No Date) //10/416 [Item] | PMBOK Guide Sixth Edition (2018) PMI/PMI/13/505 [Item The process of identifying and engaging stakeholders for the benefit of the project is iterative. Although the processes in Project Stakeholder Management are described only once, the activities of identification, prioritization, and engagement should be reviewed and updated routinely, and at least at the following times when: The project moves through different phases in its life cycle, Current stakeholders are no longer involved in the work of the project or new stakeholders become members of the project’s stakeholder community, or There are significant changes in the organization or the wider stakeholder community]

Questions.Text: During a standup meeting, team members of a software development team discovered that one of the developers is struggling to complete work due to a lack of experience with test automation. The team member is worried that this might have an impact on achieving the sprint goal. A quality assurance engineer on the team has automation testing experience and becomes aware of the problem but is busy preparing a presentation to new employees. What action would increase the chances of achieving the sprint goal?
Options: Give the automation work to the engineer as soon as the presentation is finished.
Train the developers on quality assurance and automation work.
Ask the engineer to shift their work to support the developer as soon as possible.
Remove the current activity from the sprint, and reprioritize when
Questions.Explanation: Ask the engineer to shift their work to support the developer as soon as possible Asking the engineer to shift their work to support the developer as soon as possible can increase the chances of the sprint goal being achieved. The other answer choices are incorrect. As we're speaking about achieving the current sprint goal, waiting for the engineer to finish the presentation or giving training to the team, although appropriate, are not very helpful in this case. Removing the current activity the sprint just because you cannot do it not only does not help, but also might have even a higher negative impact on the sprint goal.

Questions.Text: A developer is complaining about the time spent on daily standups, updating stories, creating tasks, and reviewing dependencies between stories instead of developing code. Other team members support the comment. What should a servant leader do next?
Options: Ask for suggestions on how to manage their time more effectively.
Reduce the interactions among team members to increase development time.
Explain the definition of an information radiator to the project team.
Acknowledge the frustration and emphasize the benefit of each activity.
Questions.Explanation: Acknowledge the frustration and emphasize the benefit of each activity A key role of a servant leader is to coach the team in agile practices and highlight the benefits of each activities and agile ceremonies and how they can help the team in completing their tasks faster. The other answer choices are incorrect. A servant leader encourages the team to have interactions to find effective solutions that can reduce the development time. The Scrum Master is the master of all Scrum practices and asking the team for suggestions is inappropriate. Explaining the definition of an information radiator is not relevant in the present scenario.

Questions.Text: A developer has completed the deliverable for a feature. The product owner is not sure how to assess the quality of the deliverable. Considering that the project is using an agile approach, what kind of tool or technique should the product owner use?
Options: Expert judgment
Decision making
Demonstrations/reviews
Checklists
Questions.Explanation: Demonstrations/reviews Demos or reviews is a part of every agile sprint that helps the product owner or customer to gain visibility on the development progress and provide feedback that facilities the team to make appropriate changes early in the project. Hence this option is the correct answer. Expert judgement, decision making and checklists are techniques that are generally a part of predictive project management approaches and hence is irrelevant in this scenario. As a result, these options are incorrect.

Questions.Text: During a kick-off meeting with the project sponsor and various project members, it became clear that it was not necessary for some participants to be present. In addition, one required expert was not aware of the meeting and was not present at the meeting. What should the project manager have done to prevent this from happening?
Options: Established a fixed communication platform to provide updates on the project to all stakeholders.
Managed stakeholder engagement by involving the stakeholders in the appropriate phases of the project.
Developed the project charter including the roles and responsibilities of all the stakeholders.
Created a responsibility matrix to notify stakeholders of the meetings they are required to attend.
Questions.Explanation: Managed stakeholder engagement by involving the stakeholders in the appropriate phases of the project Managing stakeholder engagement is the correct answer choice because it brings awareness to stakeholders so they know when their participation is required or optional. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not address the engagement of the stakeholders, which would have prevented the unawareness of the required stakeholder's attendance.

Questions.Text: During a weekly team meeting, the project manager reviewed task progress and found that a senior team member did not follow the plan. They performed tasks by personal preference, without communicating with the project manager. The senior team member is an expert and a critical resource for the project. How should the project manager address this?
Options: Discuss this with the resource personally, then update the resource management plan if necessary.
Discuss this with the functional manager, then update the resource management plan if necessary.
Discuss this with the functional manager, then update the team charter if necessary.
Discuss this with the resource personally, then update the team charter if necessary.
Questions.Explanation: Discuss this with the resource personally, then update the team charter if necessary To solve this issue, the first thing the project manager should do is talk to the resource instead of the functional manager to understand the reason and try to solve the problem before escalating. If the senior team member has a suggestion that is a better option for the project, then update the team charter if required. A team charter is a document that establishes team values, agreements, and operating guidelines.

Questions.Text: A new project manager is assigned to an ongoing agile project. The project is now starting its second 4-week iteration cycle. The project manager finds there are significant differences of opinion within the project team, leading to major setbacks for the project. What should the project manager do in order to help overcome differences and bring the project back on track?
Options: Promote conversation among team members in upcoming review meetings to help solve the problem.
Schedule a team out activity to allow people to know each other better and feel comfortable talking.
Allow the team to self-organize before the next iteration to solve the problem.
Reach out to their functional managers so they can talk to the team members and solve the problem
Questions.Explanation: Promote conversation among team members in upcoming review meetings to help solve the problem Conflict is an inevitable consequence of team interaction. The project manager should take a proactive approach to resolve this conflict. Servant leaders promote collaboration and conversation within the team and between teams. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not fully address the problem and suggest a project manager is not proactively involved in conflict resolution and promoting cohesion. The project manager needs to ensure that they work with the team to promote understanding, facilitate conflict resolution, and address the issues to address the project setbacks.

Questions.Text: A project manager does not attend the daily stand-up meetings frequently. After attending one particular meeting, the project manager noticed that the meeting was not productive. What should the project manager do to improve the situation?
Options: Analyze the dynamics of these meetings, remove impediments, and provide feedback.
Ask the team to send meeting minutes to the project manager after each daily meeting.
Start attending daily meetings to promote a better collaboration effort with the team.
Delegate the responsibility to the product owner to facilitate future daily review meetings.
Questions.Explanation: Analyze the dynamics of these meetings, remove impediments, and provide feedback Teams run their own stand-ups. The project manager should act as a servant leader, remove impediments, and coach the team as needed. This can be achieved after analyzing the dynamics of the team. The other answer choices are incorrect because they suggest the project manager take action or deflect responsibility without first analyzing the situation.

Questions.Text: A project manager is delivering a complex project for a client who has their own project manager. The client's project manager is the sole decision maker for the project but is not the delegated financial authority. For every financial decision, the client's project manager must seek approval from a higher authority. This additional step has caused significant impacts on resource management, cost, and time of project delivery. How can the project manager resolve this problem?
Options: Raise this problem in the quarterly project status report.
Add the problem to the lessons learned report.
Escalate the problem to the project steering committee.
Discuss the problem with the client's project manager.
Questions.Explanation: Escalate the problem to the project steering committee The steering committee is an advisory body that is made up of senior stakeholders and experts that provide guidance on a lot of different issues that could face companies and projects such as budgets, new endeavors, company policy, marketing strategies, and project management concerns. An authority issue should only be resolved by the steering committee. In this case, the project team cannot resolve this issue on their own, so the problem should be escalated. In this case, the client's project manager is not the delegated financial authority. The client's project manager must seek approval from a higher authority for all financial decisions, which has caused significant impacts on resource management, cost, and time of project delivery. Addressing the issue with the client's project manager would not address the core issue as the client's project manager does not have the financial authority, which is causing the issue, so it must be escalated. Adding the problem to the lessons learned report is incorrect because it does not proactively address the issue. Waiting to review the issue at the next quarterly meeting/status report is inappropriate due to the significance of the impacts. The next meeting/status report may be months away and this issue is already causing significant impacts and should be addressed as soon as possible to prevent further issues.

Questions.Text: During the execution of a project in a remote area, team members complain about the way one team member behaves after work. What should the project manager do to solve this problem?
Options: Call a meeting with the team to discuss the problem.
Plan a team-building session to discuss the problem.
Review the stakeholder's expectations and discuss this with the team.
Review the team norms and discuss this with the team.
Questions.Explanation: Review the team norms and discuss this with the team Since the behavior of the specific team member is after work hours, but it can affect the project if other team members see it as a problem, the team norms and ground rules must be discussed with the entire team. If the ground rules are not clear team members might have different expectations.

Questions.Text: A project manager is experiencing issues with the functional manager of a project team member. The functional manager has given the team member another assignment and has told the team member that the previous project should be a lower priority. What should the project manager do?
Options: Escalate the issue to senior management.
Communicate the importance of the project to the team.
Refer the functional manager to the project charter.
Request additional resources for your project.
Questions.Explanation: Refer the functional manager to the project charter The project charter will provide insights to the business case and benefits of doing the project. Thus, the project manager should refer the functional manager to the project charter. The other answer choices are incorrect. The project manager has to deal with the Functional Manager and not the team member. Hence communicating the importance of the project to the team or requesting additional resources for the project is irrelevant in this scenario. The project manager should escalate to the senior management as the last step in the process.

Questions.Text: A project manager has been hired to lead a multinational enterprise program with a geographically distributed team. There is a difference of opinion among team members about status reporting. How should the project manager resolve the issue?
Options: Adhere to the communications management plan.
Escalate the issue to the project sponsor.
Ask the team members to resolve the issue internally.
Create a new template for status reporting.
Questions.Explanation: Adhere to the communications management plan The project manager will need to emphasize to the team that the reporting should be done as per the communication management plan. Hence this option is the correct answer. The other answer choices are incorrect. Escalating the issue to the project sponsor or asking the team members to resolve the issue among themselves internally weakens the role of the project manager and does not resolve the issue. The scenario does not convey any issue with the current template of reporting. Hence creating a new template for status reporting is not required.

Questions.Text: A new project has a project team that is divided between onsite and offshore locations. Project manager observed a rising conflict between the two parts of the team during project initiation. What should project manager do?
Options: Communicate project scope to all team members.
Organize a face-to-face meeting during project kick-off.
Identify this conflict in the risk register log.
Record this conflict in the project issue log.
Questions.Explanation: Organize a face-to-face meeting during project kick-off The project manager should organize a face-to-face meeting during project kick-off phase. The project manager needs to resolve conflicts in a timely manner and in a constructive way in order to achieve a high-performing team. Communication planning becomes increasingly important in a virtual team environment. Additional time may be needed to develop protocols for resolving conflict. A meeting among both teams can start team building to enhance the team’s social relations and build a collaborative and cooperative working environment. The other answer choices are incorrect. Communicating the project scope to all team members does not resolve the rising conflict between team members. Adding rising conflict among team members to the risk register or issue log does not resolve the conflict.

Questions.Text: A project manager is required to obtain approval for requirements before proceeding to the delivery stage. The project manager realizes that the initial estimates were unrealistic and the project will take much longer to complete. What should the project manager do to mitigate the problem?
Options: Break down and re-prioritize the project deliverables to allow delivery in incremental releases.
Divide the project team into separate units, with each member working on a different component.
Survey the project team to determine which members can work overtime to meet the deadline.
Update the schedule baseline so that it is based on realistic estimates.
Questions.Explanation: Break down and re-prioritize the project deliverables to allow delivery in incremental releases In order to mitigate the problem, the project manger should break down and re-prioritize the project deliverables to allow delivery in incremental releases. Incremental releases will provide the opportunity to meet timelines with a MVP release, which can then be further enhanced over future releases to deliver the full functionality not detailed at present. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not promote incremental releases and reprioritizing. This question and rationale were developed in reference to: PMBOK Guide Sixth Edition (2018) PMI/PMI/1/56 [Item In an incremental life cycle, the deliverable is produced through a series of iterations that successively add functionality within a predetermined time frame. The deliverable contains the necessary and sufficient capability to be considered complete only after the final iteration.]

Questions.Text: A project team member informs the project manager that all items are complete and the team is ready to move on to the next iteration. The project manager reviews the iteration board and notices that there are incomplete items. The iteration ends today. What should the project manager have done to ensure that all of the work for the iteration was done on time?
Options: Validated the iteration backlog items using the definition of done (DoD)
Made the iteration cycles for the project longer
Requested team members to report progress during daily standups
Asked team members to demo their work as soon as it was ready
Questions.Explanation: Validated the iteration backlog items using the definition of done (DoD) The team should regularly validate the iteration backlog items using the definition of done[DoD] established previously to avoid delays before iteration ends. Hence this option is the correct answer. The iterations are time boxed in agile and hence cannot be increased. Hence the choice to increase the time period for the iterations is incorrect. The daily standup in principle is only a 15 minute ceremony in agile development cycle. The primary purpose of the Daily standup is to determine if the team has any obstacles or impediments in completing their tasks and not used for reporting the progress. Hence this option is incorrect. As a part of Agile development, the team members demo their increment at the end of each iteration and do not wait for all the work to be completed for performing a demo. Hence this option of asking team members to demo their work as soon as it is ready is incorrect.

Questions.Text: A project manager is concerned about delivering the planned business value because the product owner is disengaged. What should the project manager do to address the concern?
Options: Review and update the communications management plan.
Review and update the stakeholder engagement plan.
Review and update the resource management plan.
Review and update the risk management plan.
Questions.Explanation: Review and update the stakeholder engagement plan The stakeholder register should be updated to reflect the new organizational structure so the right stakeholders are engaged. Hence the option of reviewing and updating the stakeholder engagement plan is the correct choice. The other options do not address identifying right or missing stakeholders. Hence all the remaining options are incorrect.

Questions.Text: A product development project has run into design issues. As a result, the team has decided to change the approach of the product design phase from a predictive to an iterative approach. What should the project manager do to ensure a smooth transition between approaches?
Options: Schedule a workshop with the team to plan the upcoming iterations.
Schedule a meeting with the product manager to analyze user stories.
Work with the team to establish a team site and product artifacts.
Work with the product manager to define the number of iterations.
Questions.Explanation: Schedule a workshop with the team to plan the upcoming iterations It is very important that the team understands the advantages of moving to an iterative approach from the existing predictive approach. It is very important that the PM schedules a workshop to plan for the upcoming iterations at the earliest. Hence this option is the correct answer. All other options do not help with the transition between approaches. The activities are appropriate but not at this time of transition. Hence all other options are incorrect.

Questions.Text: A dedicated team member is disappointed in the performance increment within the team and raises this concern with the project manager. How should the project manager handle this situation?
Options: Escalate the issue to the human resources (HR) manager.
Meet with the team member to resolve this issue.
Verify the key performance indicators (KPIs).
Update the resource management plan.
Questions.Explanation: Verify the key performance indicators (KPIs) The project manager should verify the KPIs to gauge the performance increment within the team and address the team member's concern

Questions.Text: During a software development project, the team realizes that several features failed to comply with quality requirements. After receiving instructions to fix the issues, one team member becomes really frustrated, claiming that unclear requirements are causing the issues. Other team members argue that negligent testing is the real cause of these issues. The testers blame poor coding quality. What should the project manager do?
Options: Support the project team in a retrospective to identify the root cause of the quality issues.
Remove the most frustrated resource from the team as the confusion is impacting team motivation.
Perform a quality review in each stage of the development life cycle to identify the reason for quality issues.
Discuss the issue with the management team to identify possible solutions to improve quality.
Questions.Explanation: Support the project team in a retrospective to identify the root cause of the quality issues The project team is initially responsible for conflict resolution, but the project manager can facilitate it when required. Retrospectives can address the issue, thus the project manager should support the project team in a retrospective to identify the root cause of the quality issues.

Questions.Text: After some meetings, the team facilitator realizes the team members are working together for the first time. They are having difficulties communicating and collaborating with one another. The team facilitator wants to understand each individual`s behavior and tailor the leadership style to the team's needs. Which tools or techniques should the team facilitator use in this situation? (Choose two)
Options: Personality assessments
Weekly teleconferences
One-to-one coaching
Technical assessments
Cultural assessments
Questions.Explanation: Personality assessments and One-to-one coaching As the team is in the forming stage of the team development exercise, team collaboration plays an important role in the success of the project. Thus, it is important for the team facilitator to have to one-to-one sessions with each of the team member in order to understand them better and provide guidance in any of their weak areas that will help them improve their personality. Thus, one-to-one coaching and personality assessments are the two vital techniques that the team facilitator must use in this scenario. The other answer choices are incorrect. Assessing technical skills won`t help to understand and tailor communication requirements; weekly teleconferences also won't make the difference; and cultural assessment will not help unless there's a specific multicultural team.

Questions.Text: A project manager is beginning a new financial software project. The regulations for compliance are clear, but the requirements are not. The company is in a very competitive environment, so the customer needs results as soon as possible. Which project life cycle should the project manager choose for this project?
Options: Iterative
Predictive
Dynamic
Incremental
Questions.Explanation: Incremental When the requirements are not clear and the customer needs results as soon as possible, the most suitable method is to use an incremental life cycle. A deliverable with an incremental life cycle is created through a series of iterations that incrementally increase functionality within a set time Period. Only after the last iteration does the deliverable possess the essential and sufficient capacity to be termed complete.

Questions.Text: A team is transitioning from a predictive to an agile approach. Which two obstacles are the team most likely to encounter? (Choose two)
Options: Management provides freedom for the team to decide.
Management continues to control the processes of delivery.
Management would like the team to learn and adapt.
Management support is lacking for team empowerment.
Management expects the team to keep their performance level.
Questions.Explanation: Management continues to control the processes of delivery and Management support is lacking for team empowerment During the transformation from a predictive to an agile approach, the common impediments the team will face are the lack of empowerment and the management style of controlling the process of delivery. A significant part of any Agile transformation is enabling an organization to move away from a command and control culture to an environment that is more focused on collaboration and cultivation. Transitioning from predictive development approaches to adaptive approaches and then to using agile methods is more than just stating that the organization will now be agile. It entails shifting the mindset starting at the executive level throughout the organization. Organizational policies, ways of working, reporting structure, and attitude should all be aligned. Some agile approaches entail iterations that are 1 to 2 weeks in duration with a demonstration of the accomplishments at the end of each iteration. The project team is very engaged with the planning for each iteration. The project team will determine the scope they can achieve based on a prioritized backlog, estimate the work involved, and work collaboratively throughout the iteration to develop the scope.

Questions.Text: A 5-year highway construction project is in its third year of execution. The project manager received a complaint from an activist group in the neighborhood. What should the project manager have done to avoid this issue?
Options: Ask the project communications team to address the complaint.
Request the formal notification complaint from the activist group
Identify the neighborhood representatives in the communications management plan.
Identify the risks associated with the highway construction project.
Questions.Explanation: Identify the neighborhood representatives in the communications management plan Project stakeholder identification and analyses is part of the project communication management process.

Questions.Text: A project manager asks one of their team members to prepare a monthly report in a format supplied by the CEO. The day before the meeting, the project manager reviews the report, which is not formatted as requested. What should the project manager have done to avoid this situation?
Options: Asked for the report from the team member earlier to ensure it was in the requested format.
Requested more detailed requirements about the format from the CEO.
Employed the interactive communications model to ensure the team member understood requirements.
Modified the communications management plan to include the new report.
Questions.Explanation: Employed the interactive communications model to ensure the team member understood requirements As part of the communication process, the sender is responsible for the transmission of the message, ensuring the information being communicated is clear and complete, and confirming the message is correctly interpreted. The other answer choices are incorrect because they do not address the executive expectation and understanding of the task by the team member.

Questions.Text: A project manager realizes that some newly hired team members have not been as productive as hoped, which is putting the project at risk. What should the project manager do to resolve this issue?
Options: Provide additional training to the new team members.
Conduct job shadowing within the project team.
Institute project milestone completion incentives.
Tell the team that productivity must improve.
Questions.Explanation: Conduct job shadowing within the project team If project team members lack the necessary management or technical skills, such skills can be developed as part of the project work. Scheduled training takes place as stated in the resource management plan. Unplanned training, such as job shadowing, takes place as a result of observation, conversation, or project performance appraisals conducted during the management of the project team. Job shadowing can quickly bring knowledge and discover inefficiencies in work performance. The other answer choices are incorrect because they don't identify the source of the lack of productivity.

Questions.Text: An external stakeholder has the potential to have a major impact on a project. What approach should the project manager take with this stakeholder?
Options: Communicate with the stakeholder according to the stakeholder engagement and communications management plans.
Request feedback from the stakeholder so that the project meets the stakeholder's expectations and can be delivered as planned.
Add the stakeholder to the risk register and quantify the level of impact that the stakeholder could have.
Inform the stakeholder of the key project benefits and keep them updated on the project's milestone achievements.
Questions.Explanation: Communicate with the stakeholder according to the stakeholder engagement and communications management plans Managing stakeholder engagement helps to ensure that stakeholders clearly understand the project goals, objectives, benefits, and risks for the project, as well as how their contribution will enhance project success. The stakeholder engagement plan is a component of the project management plan that identifies the strategies and actions required to promote productive involvement of stakeholders in decision making and execution.

Questions.Text: A company is creating a new application for its users. The agile team completed the initiating stage and is performing iterations to complete planning and execution. However, a stakeholder wants to change the order of the deliverables to demonstrate new aspects of the app to stakeholders. How should the project manager address this situation?
Options: Implement sprint planning with the team and the stakeholder to ensure the right priorities are assigned.
Use key performance indicators (KPIs) to demonstrate to the stakeholder that the project is progressing on schedule.
Ask the stakeholder for a change request and submit it to the change control board (CCB).
Explain to the stakeholder that modifying the schedule now will increase the delivery risk.
Questions.Explanation: Implement sprint planning with the team and the stakeholder to ensure the right priorities are assigned Agile methodology values the “ability to respond easily to change over following a plan.” On an agile initiative, the project manager don’t freeze the requirements, and only a lightweight process is needed to change them. Changing requirements harness a competitive agile advantage where changing requirements, customer collaboration, and reliance on conversation take precedence over documentation. On a waterfall project, the requirements and the implementation plan are frozen before implementation and any changes after that trigger a formal change process.

Questions.Text: The project manager needs to survey the necessary parties to reach consensus and build a common vision for the project. To achieve better results, who should be engaged in the development of a common vision strategy?
Options: The client and the delivery team
The delivery team and the project sponsor
The project team and the stakeholders
The project owner and the client
Questions.Explanation: The project team and the stakeholders The project manager should engage the project team and the stakeholders when developing a common vision strategy because the team members must carry out the tasks that impact the stakeholders. The project manager must balance the conflicting and competing goals of the project stakeholders in order to achieve consensus. In this context, consensus means that the relevant stakeholders support the project decisions and actions taken by the team members. Also, involving stakeholders during initiation creates a shared understanding of success criteria. It also increases the likelihood of accepting the deliverables of the project.

Questions.Text: An email from a project manager to a team member is accidentally distributed to the entire global project team. It contains harsh feedback on a recent quality incident, which could have been avoided by closer attention to detail. What should the project manager do next?
Options: Await responses and feedback from the team about the email prior to responding.
Ask the team to attend a virtual meeting and discuss the email as a group.
Create a special follow-up apology email and send it to the entire team.
Send a special follow-up apology email to the intended recipient.
Questions.Explanation: Ask the team to attend a virtual meeting and discuss the email as a group Transparency is important in a team and now that the team is aware of the situation, it can be used as an opportunity to discuss lessons learned, hence the project manager should ask the team to attend a virtual meeting and discuss the email as a group. The other answer choices are incorrect. Awaiting responses and feedback does not correct the mistake and the unintended public shaming; creating a special follow-up apology email and sending it to the entire team is not the best choice as apology is not going to provide an opportunity to learn from the mistakes; sending a special follow-up apology email to the intended recipient does not address the unintended public shaming with the group.

Questions.Text: A collocated project team is facing communication issues because team members arrive and leave at different hours of the day. At a recent weekly meeting, several members were absent. What is the best way for the project manager to ensure the team is on the same level of communication?
Options: Encourage team members to work the same hours.
Discuss the meeting with the missing team members.
Create an information radiator for public display.
Schedule team meetings during overlapping core hours.
Questions.Explanation: Schedule team meetings during overlapping core hours The project manager should schedule team meetings during overlapping core hours to allow team members to attend and receive the same message. The team is colocated, but some members arrive and leave at different hours, so the best option would be to schedule meetings at a time that works for all team members. The other answer choices are incorrect. Encouraging team members to work the same hours does not ensure that they will be available for the meeting. It may also be impossible for the team members to change their schedules. Creating an information radiator will provide details, but the focus is at the summary level only and does not help to address the absentees. Discussing the meeting with the missing team members is incorrect because it is redundant, inefficient, and does not address the issue.

Questions.Text: As part of the project sprint review, stakeholders appear uninformed and are unable to provide their agreement to move the project forward. What should the project manager do first to ensure stakeholders are able to approve the project deliverables?
Options: Review the communications management plan.
Perform a stakeholder analysis.
Include the stakeholders in daily standup meetings.
Colocate the team.
Questions.Explanation: Review the communications management plan "Review the communications management plan" is the best choice as the project manager should review the project communication plan to ensure that the communication needs of the stakeholders are being met. Since the communication plan is developed at the beginning of the project and is based on the needs of the stakeholders, during the course of the project it would be important to first review the plan to ensure it is sound and adjust as needed. "Colocate the team" is not going to address the gap identified/described in the stem as Colocation refers to the team being at same location for better collaboration. "Include the stakeholders..." Is not the correct choice to include stakeholder for daily standup as the objective and target audience are different. "Perform a stakeholder analysis" is not going to resolve the communication issue.
